entries.unlock: Purchase from Ox and Cadence for 8 HC.
entries.englishGameplaySummary: Three times the charm. Gunies, unlike their non-gun counterparts, have no problem with death wishes.
entries.englishEffectHighlights: Shooting an enemy causes a mote of blue energy (a wish) to appear above their head. Accumulating three 'wishes' on a single enemy summons a muscular Gunie (Gun Genie) to punch them for massive damage.; Shooting an enemy summons a Gunie to punch them for large damage. Each shot summons a gunie, with no need to accumulate three wishes.
entries.englishSynergyHighlights: Sweetness and Light If the player also has Magic Sweet, Magic Lamp fires three projectiles at a time. The shots deal 25% of their original damage, but the genie deals full damage.
entries.englishUsageNotes: The Ammonomicon entry is a reference to the Disney film Aladdin, where one of the rules of the Genie character was that he won't kill anybody.; The Sweetness and Light synergy is a reference to an English idiom.

entries.englishGameplaySummary: This modified shotgun has been refitted for one-handed use. The Winchester shotgun packs the same punch as a regular shotgun, but chambers more efficiently.
entries.englishSynergyHighlights: Not So Sawed-Off If the player has Buzzkill, Buzzkill's saw blades are linked with lightning.; The Winchester is in the SHOTGUN gun class, meaning that it benefits from the Shotgun Affinity synergy.
entries.englishUsageNotes: The Winchester is wielded by Blue Shotgun Kin.; The quote "Better Than A Box Of Roses" is a reference to a scene in the movie Terminator 2: Judgement Day, where the Terminator pulls a Winchester out of a box full of roses.

entries.unlock: Purchase from Ox and Cadence for 1 HC.
entries.englishGameplaySummary: A favorite of organized criminals and Hollow Points, this gun has accumulated nicknames over its hundreds of years of use. Known affectionately as the "Tommy Gun," the "Trench Broom", the "Chicago Typewriter", and the "Teetotaller's Thursday."
entries.englishSynergyHighlights: Future Gangster If the player has Bionic Leg, Laser Sight, Shock Rounds, or Nanomachines, the Thompson Sub-Machinegun fires lasers. Each laser does 6.5 damage instead of the normal 5 damage.
entries.englishUsageNotes: The Thompson Sub-Machinegun is wielded by Hollowpoints.; "Myeah, See!" references a line spoken by Edward G. Robinson's gangster character in the 1931 film Little Caesar.

entries.englishGameplaySummary: An overcharged anti-loitering and mob dispersal device. Forces enemies to forget their purpose and conviction.
entries.englishEffectHighlights: Rapidly fires piercing sound waves that grow in width the further they travel.; Waves have a 1% chance to stun enemies for 3 seconds on impact.
entries.englishSynergyHighlights: Banshee Cry If the player has Dueling Laser, the Screecher fires a cone of three waves per shot.
entries.englishUsageNotes: The sound waves destroy chests very quickly; it is not recommended to check for mimics using this gun.; The sound waves also crack the walls of secret rooms very quickly, making it a great, ammo efficient gun for locating them.

entries.englishGameplaySummary: Darts explode on reload. A favorite of the adventurer Frifle, who appreciates traditional weaponry but isn't opposed to a few combustible upgrades.
entries.englishUsageNotes: Triple Sticky If the player also has Triple Crossbow, Sticky Crossbow will fire three bolts per shot. Triple Crossbow's triple shot will explode upon hitting an enemy.; If the player has a passive allowing bullets to pierce, the explosion will occur where the bolt struck.

entries.unlock: Purchase from Trorc for 10 HC.
entries.englishGameplaySummary: An extremely powerful rifle. Banned in some sectors, its ease of use caused it to be the weapon of choice for thousands of unskilled marksmen. Only handicapped by its long chambering time. Some say that it will fire faster if you put it in your backpack after every shot...
entries.englishSynergyHighlights: 360 Yes Scope If the player also has Scope, spinning 360 degrees before firing grants a 3-second buff that makes the next shot deal 50% more damage.; This can be activated multiple times, providing a stacking 1.5x damage multiplier for each spin. It caps at 6 rotations.
entries.englishUsageNotes: Due to its slow rate of fire it is easy to fire another gun between A.W.P. shots without A.W.P. downtime.; A.W.P is an acronym for Arctic Warfare Police.

entries.englishGameplaySummary: The Zorgun is a mass-produced all-purpose machine gun. Its eggshell design allows it to be stored easily in intergalactic cruise ship compartments.
entries.englishEffectHighlights: Rapidly fires 600 projectiles.; The last shot of each magazine fires either an RPG rocket, a Grenade Launcher grenade, or a Bullet Bore drill.
entries.englishSynergyHighlights: Ancient Aliens If the player has Alien Engine, Zorgun gets three special shots at the end of each magazine instead of one. Alien Engine will also fire Zorgun bullets.; Mr. Shadow If the player has Shadow Bullets, the Zorgun's shadow bullets become random explosives.
entries.englishUsageNotes: The Zorgun is a reference to the ZF1 weapon sold by the villain Zorg in the film The Fifth Element. The Ammonomicon quote "Don't Push The Red Button" is a reference to the ZF1's red self destruct button in the film.; The entry for the Zorgun in the Physical Ammonomicon has a small doodle of a strangely dressed Bullet Kin and a tablet styled after a Shotgun Kin. These are references to Zorg and the Elemental Stones from The Fifth Element respectively.

entries.englishGameplaySummary: Even mentioning guns in the Gungeon can have strange effects. This is an old idiom that has been given physical form through simple repetition. Note: Words are the Gungeon's second language, so slight inaccuracies may occur.
entries.englishEffectHighlights: Fires blue fish.; Fish have a 20% chance to stun enemies, and leave pools of water when flopping onto the ground that are capable of extinguishing chest fuses and the fireplace.
entries.englishSynergyHighlights: Like Shooting Fish If the player has Backup Gun, a fish pops out of the barrel and fires bullets. The last shot of each magazine fires a fish that flops around on the ground and fires at enemies.; One Fish Two Fish The Barrel fires two fish in a helical pattern, one of which is red and sets enemies on fire.
entries.englishUsageNotes: The gun itself as well as its flavor text are a reference to the idiom "Like shooting fish in a barrel" describing an effortless or simple action, with guaranteed success.; One Fish Two Fish Is a clear reference to a book of a similar name written by Dr. Seuss.

entries.englishGameplaySummary: The classic Bow will never go out of style.
entries.englishEffectHighlights: Charges to shoot arrows.; If fired fully charged, its arrows will move faster, deal more damage, and pierce enemies.
entries.englishSynergyHighlights: Cerebral Bros If the player also has Bullet Bore, Bullet Bore turns red and fires two drills at a time.; Diazepam Increases damage by 50% while the Bow is held.
entries.englishUsageNotes: Arrowkin wield the Bow.; The charge effect is likely a reference to the game "Titan Souls".

stats.value: Assuming each bullet only hits once.

entries.englishGameplaySummary: Fires ricocheting musket balls. This most ancient of pistols has settled many a lovers' quarrel. This particular flintlock would be fairly useless in a duel, as the ricochet would probably kill several bystanders.
entries.englishSynergyHighlights: Dead Prez If the player also has Derringer, Derringer fires bouncing Dueling Pistol shots, and Dueling Pistol fires a wide spread of 4 bullets alongside its normal shots.; Death Square If the player also has Dueling Laser, damaging enemies with the Dueling Pistol rapidly recharges the Dueling Laser.
entries.englishUsageNotes: The Hell Singing synergy is likely a reference to Van Helsing, the vampire hunter created by Bram Stoker.; The Dead Prez synergy is likely a reference to the death of Abraham Lincoln, who was shot with a Derringer, and the Dueling Pistol likely is referencing the many duels that Andrew Jackson had in his lifetime.

entries.englishGameplaySummary: A water gun might seem to be a poor weapon for Gungeoneering, and in most cases that is true. But a well-placed blast of water can douse a raging inferno; a soaked enemy can be electrified; a dazed foe can be pushed into the abyss. In the Gungeon, creativity is key to victory.
entries.englishEffectHighlights: This water stream can extinguish the fireplace, put out Chest fuses, and clean off hidden Rainbow Chests.; Because the Mega Douser uses the Water damage type, it deals 2x damage to Muzzle Wisps and Muzzle Flares, and does not ignite Coalers.
entries.englishSynergyHighlights: Get Equipped With -B- Enables Bubble Lead mode on the Megahand, which fires lingering 12 damage bubbles while uncharged. Unlike other forms of the Megahand, this form will constantly fire uncharged shots while charging up to fire its energy blast. Modes can be switched by reloading the Megahand with a full magazine.; Like the Mega Douser, the Bubble Lead mode of the Megahand leaves water that can extinguish chest fuses and the fireplace. It also uses the Water damage type, granting the aforementioned bonuses against Muzzle Wisps and Coalers.
entries.englishUsageNotes: The name and appearance of the gun reference Super Soaker, a brand of toy water guns.; More specifically, its shape and color scheme are based on the original Super Soaker 50.

entries.englishGameplaySummary: One of the earliest examples of trigger fired projectiles.
entries.englishUsageNotes: There is a delay between the shot being fired and the auto reload which can be canceled with a manual reload.; If playing in coop mode and the Hunter dies whilst holding the Crossbow, it will disappear. It will not disappear if others hold it.

entries.englishGameplaySummary: One of the first energy weapons created in the Gungeon. When lightning struck an old gunsmith's gun-rack, he decided to sand it down into a gun. The result is crude, as far as energy weapons go, but effective.
entries.englishEffectHighlights: Quickly fires small balls of electricity.; Any pools of water that these balls pass over will be electrified, dealing considerable damage to enemies.
entries.englishSynergyHighlights: Alistair's Ladder If the player has Shock Rounds, Thunderclap fires two parallel balls of lightning with every shot, linking the bullets together in a ladder pattern.; If the synergy is obtained through Lich's Eye Bullets, the Shock Rounds effect will not be applied.
entries.englishUsageNotes: Altered: Diazepam Prior to the Advanced Gungeons & Draguns Update, this gun was included in Diazepam.; The damaging field while reloading was added in A Farewell to Arms.

entries.englishGameplaySummary: Shoots bees. A beehive is nature's gun! Give it a good shake and let the bullets fly.
entries.englishEffectHighlights: Fires bees that label=home in on and damage enemies. Bees deal 3 damage on impact and apply a lingering 'swarm' effect that deals 1 damage right away, and deals 1 damage every second for 2 more seconds.; The initial 3 damage impact of each bee is affected by the players damage stat, but the lingering swarm damage is not.
entries.englishSynergyHighlights: Apiary If the player has Box, Bee Hive turns into an apiary and automatically regenerates ammo over time.; BEES If the player has Honeycomb or Bumbullets, while Bee Hive is held, projectile size is doubled, damage is increased by 25%, and rate of fire is decreased by 34%.
entries.englishUsageNotes: If the player feeds the Bee Hive and the RPG to a regular Muncher, it will always spit out a Stinger.; Bees will not home in on enemies that are not necessary for room clear.

entries.englishGameplaySummary: The AK-47 is in many ways the gun against which all other guns are judged. This affordable and reliable piece of hardware has proven itself in nearly any terrain or situation. Desert, jungle, snow, and dungeon were all accounted for in its timeless design. It can even fire underwater.
entries.englishSynergyHighlights: AK-47 (Island Forme) If the player has Orange, pressing reload with a full magazine toggles Island Forme. Island Forme AK-47 fires oranges, which deal more damage than regular AK-47 shots. Each orange deals 7 damage. If the Orange is eaten while the AK-47 is in Island Forme, it will remain in Island Forme, but if the player switches away from Island Forme, they will not be able to switch back. It can be reloaded by any means except manual reload. Such as firing when empty.; Regular Old Guns If the player also has Regular Shotgun, both guns fire four times as fast, but their damage is decreased by 25%.
entries.englishUsageNotes: The AK-47 is wielded by some Bullet Kin, Tankers, and Hollowpoints.; If the player feeds the AK-47 and either the Flame Hand or Megahand to a regular Muncher, it will always spit out a Machine Fist.

entries.unlock: Purchase from Ox and Cadence for 25 HC.
entries.englishGameplaySummary: Chain-fires homing rockets. A legendary weapon manufactured by Zilla Industries. This one was created bespoke for a powerful warrior, whose name has been lost to shadow.
entries.englishEffectHighlights: Rapidly fires tightly 220 rockets that create small explosions upon hitting an enemy.; The damage dealt by these rockets (both their impact damage and explosive damage) bypass the Boss DPS cap, allowing this gun to kill bosses extremely quickly.
entries.englishSynergyHighlights: Hail To The King If the player also has Oiled Cylinder, Yari Launcher's magazine size is increased by 50% and its damage is increased by 20%.; Shadow Warrior If the player also has Zilla Shotgun, when either gun is held, the player gains an orbiting Demon Head that fires on empty magazine reload. Reduces the guns' spread by 60%, increasing their accuracy.
entries.englishUsageNotes: The gun is a reference to the rocket launcher in the 2013 reboot of Shadow Warrior.; The orbiting Demon Head gained through the Shadow Warrior synergy is likely a reference to one of the game's weapons, a demon head that shoots a laser out of its mouth.

entries.englishGameplaySummary: Extremely rapid fire. Used by a wiggly hero in the early days of the Gungeon.
entries.englishEffectHighlights: Rapidly fires invisible bullets.; Jammed enemies and bosses hit by the Heck Blaster will revert to normal.
entries.englishSynergyHighlights: Dr Worm If the player also has Drum Clip, Heck Blaster regains 1 ammo every 3 seconds.; Great Queen Ant If the player also has Gungeon Ant, Gungeon Ant becomes a queen and shoots rocket ants.
entries.englishUsageNotes: This gun is useful in high runs, as it gives players a counter to the high spawn rates of Jammed enemies. This gun has no effect on the Lord of the Jammed, however, and reverted enemies will only drop the usual amount of money upon death.; Gunjurers cannot absorb Heck Blaster's bullets.

entries.englishGameplaySummary: Extremely high knockback. A poor facsimile of the Gun That Can Kill The Past. Seeking an alternate route to past modification, time travelers would use these guns to eliminate their past selves and take their place. Unfortunately, without the power of the true Gun, this always resulted in ironic consequences.
entries.englishEffectHighlights: Fires a shotgun blast of 6 short-range projectiles with high knockback.; Deals 20% less damage to bosses.
entries.englishSynergyHighlights: Iron Stance If the player also has Heavy Boots, the gun's damage and knockback are increased by 20%, its shot speed is increased by 50%, and its reload time is decreased by 20%.; Thrown For A Bloop If the player also has Bullet Time, when an active item is used while holding the Blooper, another Blooper orbits the player and continually fires at enemies for the duration of the active item's effect.
entries.englishUsageNotes: Gunjurers cannot catch the Blooper's shots.; Blooper is a reference to the 2012 time travel film Looper. The gun's tagline, "Close Your Bloop", is a reference to the movie's tagline, "Close Your Loop".

entries.englishGameplaySummary: Fires grenades that can bounce. A difficult weapon to use effectively, but powerful in the right hands. The distinctive noise this gun makes is beloved by Pinheads.
entries.englishEffectHighlights: Fires explosive grenades.; Grenades skip across the ground, and are able to ricochet off of a wall or object once.
entries.englishSynergyHighlights: Terminated, Too If the player has Potion of Lead Skin, the grenades fire 8 bullets in all directions upon exploding that set enemies on fire.
entries.englishUsageNotes: This gun is based on the real-world M79 Grenade Launcher, which was developed as a higher powered and more accurate substitute for the US Military's rifle-barrel grenades.; The item quote "Fwomp!" is a reference to the real-life weapon's distinctive report when fired.

entries.englishGameplaySummary: This powerful laser was designed to carve moon rock at high speed during terraforming projects.
entries.englishEffectHighlights: Fires a laser beam that reflects off of walls or obstacles once.
entries.englishSynergyHighlights: Jumping... Is Useless If the player also has Quad Laser, four Moonscraper laser beams shoot from each side of Quad Laser's bullets. Doubles Quad Laser's shot speed and triples its reload time.; Klobbering Time If the player also has Klobbe, its shots are doubled.
entries.englishUsageNotes: This gun is a reference to the Moonraker laser rifle from the 1979 James Bond movie Moonraker, as well as the Moonraker Laser weapon from the Nintendo 64 game, GoldenEye 007.; The synergy Land Among The Stars references the idiom, "Shoot for the moon; even if you miss, you’ll land among the stars."

entries.englishGameplaySummary: Charge to clear rooms. A Gungeon classic, and one of the first to be imported through the Gungeon Acquisitions Dept. The BSG's powerful blast will make short work of nearly any Gundead.
entries.englishSynergyHighlights: Big Shotgun Gun If the player has Big Shotgun, it fires five projectiles instead of three.; In The Mood! If the player has Thunderclap or Strafe Gun, shot speed is increased by 25% and the player's movement speed is slightly increased.
entries.englishUsageNotes: Items that increase shot speed (such as Rocket-Powered Bullets) will allow projectiles to reach their maximum distance quicker and thus detonate in less time.; Projectiles will detonate instantly if they come in contact with a black hole created by either the Black Hole Gun or Singularity.

entries.englishGameplaySummary: The cooler bullet brother's handgun. The barrel has 17 notches carved along its length, and somewhat incongruously, a picture of a smiling Bullet Kin. Anyone who uses this handgun would look pretty cool.
entries.englishEffectHighlights: Increases Coolness by 3 while in the players inventory, decreasing the cooldown of active items and increasing the chance of items dropping upon clearing a room.
entries.englishSynergyHighlights: Trigger Twins If the player also has Smiley's Revolver, ammo capacity is doubled and magazine size is increased by 50% for all guns, and the two guns will be dual wielded.; While Lich's Eye Bullets doesn't give the dual wielding portion of the synergy, it will give the 2x ammo capacity and 50% magazine size increase.
entries.englishUsageNotes: The pickup line "Someone Loses An Eye", could be a reference to the latter part of the phrase "It's all fun and games until someone loses an eye."; The corresponding pickup line of Smiley's Revolver, "All Smiles," is likely a corruption of the first part of the phrase.

entries.englishGameplaySummary: A powerful, unwieldy handgun. A favorite more for its image than its efficiency, the Dungeon Eagle is capable of delivering massive damage. Not terribly accurate, but highly memorable.
entries.englishSynergyHighlights: Don't Hoot the Messenger If the player also has Owl, halves time required to fire a charged shot.; Reload Roll If the player also has Easy Reload Bullets, dodge rolling reloads six bullets and dodge rolling range is slightly increased while holding the weapon.
entries.englishUsageNotes: The name Dungeon Eagle is a reference to the real-world 'Desert Eagle' gun.; The description of the weapon refers to the Desert Eagle's popularity in American culture and the weapon's iconic reputation despite its shortcomings.

entries.englishGameplaySummary: Just because children are young, doesn't mean they don't have pasts they would like to change. Perhaps some young soul wished to make sure his or her sibling took the blame for spilling grape juice on the couch. Much less worthy causes have been championed in the Gungeon.
entries.englishUsageNotes: The Dart Gun may be a reference to the Nerf Maverick REV-6, a toy foam dart blaster that has the same shape, similar color pattern (albeit purple instead of yellow), and also holds six darts.; The Dart Gun is a replacement toy for the Cultist, after they lost their previous toy blaster (the Light Gun) down a pit."After a clumsy child of the Gungeon accidentally dropped this toy into a pit, they were forced to amuse themselves with the Dart Gun." - The Ammonomicon description of the Light Gun

entries.englishGameplaySummary: The most famous rifle in history. This gun's usefulness and reliability have become legend. Though not native to the Gungeon, its arrival seemed to be expected by the Gundead.
entries.englishSynergyHighlights: Diazepam Increases damage by 50% while the M1 is held.; M1 Multi-Tool If the player also has Scope, Drum Clip, Ammo Belt, Backpack, or Utility Belt, M1's reload time is decreased by 20%, its magazine size is increased by 50%, and its maximum ammo is increased by 80%. Movement speed is also increased while M1 is held.
entries.englishUsageNotes: Although classified as semi-automatic, the M1 is functionally automatic, as its rate of fire does not benefit from tapping the fire button instead of holding it down.; There are several real guns with the name 'M1', however the shape (such as the band around the rifle's forend), distinctive pinging reload sound, and Ammonomicon entry of the weapon (Coupled with the fact that it is referred to as the 'Grand Rifle' in the Apple Arcade version of Exit the Gungeon) suggest that this rifle is the M1 Garand, a famous semi-automatic rifle that served as the mainstay of the US Army in World War 2.

entries.englishGameplaySummary: This ancient tool is theorized to have been used in bizarre pagan rituals involving spiders and flame.
entries.englishSynergyHighlights: Careful Iteration If the player has Casey, Casey becomes spiked and fires a spread of 6 nails each time it is swung.; Hammer and Nail If the player also has H4mmer, maximum ammo is increased by 50% for both guns. H4mmer additionally gets 5 hammer shots at the end of every magazine and spins a wood beam around the player when reloading an empty magazine.
entries.englishUsageNotes: This gun is among those Gunsling King and Manservantes can challenge the player to clear a room with.; The Ammonomicon entry is a reference to the 1990s horror film Arachnophobia.

entries.englishGameplaySummary: After a clumsy child of the Gungeon accidentally dropped this toy into a pit, they were forced to amuse themselves with the Dart Gun.
entries.englishEffectHighlights: Fires an incredibly fast zap of white light capable of piercing through enemies.; The final bullet in each magazine is a mallard duck, which automatically seeks out enemies to attack them. This duck is capable of piercing once, and has extremely tight homing.
entries.englishSynergyHighlights: EGG MURDERER If the player has The Scrambler, the duck will periodically shoot two Scrambler shots perpendicular to itself as it travels.; Flak Bullets stops this synergy from working.
entries.englishUsageNotes: This gun is a reference to the NES Zapper.; The duck being shot is a reference to Duck Hunt, one of the most well-known games to use the NES Zapper.

entries.unlock: Purchase from Ox and Cadence for 5 HC.
entries.englishGameplaySummary: Contains junk mail and one untrustworthy package. It was a wise and hopeful Gungeoneer who observed that mailboxes share the same basic shape of guns. When they sought to change their past, they wondered whether any gun shaped object would operate as guns do, within the Gungeons walls.
entries.englishSynergyHighlights: Flat Stanley If the player also has Box, magazine size is reduced by 50% and reload time is reduced by 33%, allowing packages to be fired more often.; Junk Mail If the player also has Magazine Rack, activating the Magazine Rack causes Mailbox to only shoot out packages.
entries.englishUsageNotes: This gun is the only way to get the Kill a boss after covering it with glitter achievement, acquired by killing a boss after covering it in glitter.; Many of the final shot's effects are references to real mail-related attacks:

entries.englishGameplaySummary: An abomination. This dark weapon was assembled from a fallen adventurer's spinal column and an AK-47 frame. When Nuign was first exiled from his homeland, he was heartbroken. In his pain, he turned to the Gungeon.
entries.englishEffectHighlights: Rapidly fires homing ghost-shaped bullets that can pierce once.; When close together, these bullets form chain links between each other that damage enemies.
entries.englishSynergyHighlights: Dawn of the Gundead If the player also has Zombie Bullets, the chance for VertebraeK-47 ammo to be refunded upon missing is doubled.; Jolly Roger If the player also has Skull Spitter, VertebraeK-47 can freeze enemies. Skull Spitter fires three skulls per shot, and skulls are linked like VertebraeK-47 shots.
entries.englishUsageNotes: The weapon's name is a portmanteau of 'vertebrae' and 'AK-47'. Vertebrae are bones which a spine consists of, and the AK-47 is an assault rifle.; The description of the gun is a reference to the Jake Clover game, Nuign Spectre.

entries.englishGameplaySummary: The ancient design fires simple lead projectiles from a moderately-sized clip. In the right hands, this weapon is a force to be reckoned with.
entries.englishSynergyHighlights: Behold! If the player also has Com4nd0, Eye of the Beholster, Void Marshal, Machine Pistol, and Trank Gun (the weapons the Beholster uses in game), when the Eye of the Beholster is held, the other five guns rotate around the player and shoot at nearby enemies, still using up ammo from each gun. Obtainable through the Beholster shrine, although it can be activated without.; Mmmmmmmmm MMMMmm! If the player also has M1 and M16, increases the damage, maximum ammo, and fire rate of all three guns by 25%.
entries.englishUsageNotes: This gun is one of the six guns wielded by the Beholster.; As it is held by the Beholster, it can be placed on the Beholster Shrine.

entries.englishGameplaySummary: Gungeonologists have long debated what is truly the worst gun in the Gungeon. More Gungeoneers have perished with Klobbes in their inventory than any other gun. Coincidence?
entries.englishSynergyHighlights: Klobbering Time If the player also has AU Gun or Moonscraper, the Klobbe is doubled and fires two shots at a time.; No Odd Job If the player also has Makarov, the two guns are dual-wielded. Their damage is increased by 20% and spread is increased by 60%, decreasing accuracy.
entries.englishUsageNotes: This gun is among those Gunsling King and Manservantes can challenge the player to clear a room with.; This gun is a reference to the Klobb from GoldenEye 007 (itself based on the real-life Škorpion), a notoriously bad gun with similar weaknesses as the Klobbe.

entries.englishGameplaySummary: Compact, but highly accurate, the Void Marshal is a favorite of smugglers and rogues across the galaxy.
entries.englishSynergyHighlights: Behold! If the player also has Com4nd0, M1911, Eye of the Beholster, Machine Pistol, and Trank Gun (the weapons the Beholster uses in game), increases damage by 30%, magazine size by 50%, spread by 50%, decreases reload time by 33%, and slows down enemy bullets by 20% when one of the guns is held. When the Eye of the Beholster is held, the other five guns rotate around the player and shoot at nearby enemies, still using up ammo from each gun randomly. Obtainable through the Beholster shrine, although it can be activated without.
entries.englishUsageNotes: This gun is one of the six guns wielded by the Beholster.; As it is held by the Beholster, it can be placed on the Beholster Shrine.

entries.englishGameplaySummary: Fires tears. This grotesque gun is made of eyeballs bound together in a gun-like shape. Tears are a surprisingly effective projectile for exploring dungeons!
entries.englishEffectHighlights: Shoots tears that pierce enemies and leave puddles of water on collision.; The direction of these tears is affected by the player's momentum, meaning that tears angle in the direction the player is moving.
entries.englishSynergyHighlights: Tears of Blood If the player has Bloody Eye, Tear Jerker's shots turn red and inflict slow.; Wrath Of The Blam If the player has Directional Pad, Tear Jerker is replaced with a crying bullet head that can only fire in cardinal directions but deals double damage.
entries.englishUsageNotes: Because the Tear Jerker uses the Water damage type, it deals 2x damage to Muzzle Wisps and Muzzle Flares, and does not ignite Coalers.; The Tear Jerker also puts out decorative torches and burning skeletons.

entries.englishGameplaySummary: The younger bullet brother's handgun. It is unhelpfully labelled with an 'S'. Shopkeepers found Smiley's good nature disarming, often giving him the advantage when negotiating prices.
entries.englishEffectHighlights: Decreases shop prices by 15% while in the players inventory.
entries.englishSynergyHighlights: Trigger Twins If the player also has Shades's Revolver, ammo capacity is doubled and magazine size is increased by 50% for all guns, and the two guns are dual wielded.; While Lich's Eye Bullets doesn't give the dual wielding portion of the synergy, it will give the 2x ammo capacity and 50% magazine size increase.
entries.englishUsageNotes: This item's description references both the name and effect of Disarming Personality.; The entry for Smiley's Revolver in the Physical Ammonomicon has a small doodle of Smiley.

entries.englishGameplaySummary: The Megahand is a relic of the Ten Days' War against the robots. The most powerful robot masters would modify it for their own styles of combat. This one is basic, but it still works well.
entries.englishEffectHighlights: Fires small yellow pellets that deal 6 damage.; Charges up to fire a powerful blue energy blast that deals 45 damage and knocks enemies back.
entries.englishSynergyHighlights: If the player has certain items or guns, reloading with a full magazine switches the Megahand to a different mode. If the corresponding item or gun is dropped while in an alternative mode, the mode is still kept until the player next reloads a full magazine.; Get Equipped With -A- Air Shooter, which fires piercing and 400 tornadoes when uncharged.
entries.englishUsageNotes: This gun is a reference to Mega Man's arm cannon, called the Mega Buster.; The tooltip references how in Mega Man through Mega Man 3, the Mega Buster was represented in menus by the Symbol P.

entries.englishGameplaySummary: A cannon of serious power! This launches cannonballs that cannot be stopped by anything but a solid wall. The Serious Cannon was brought to the Gungeon by Sam "Serious" Stone.
entries.englishSynergyHighlights: Black Flag If the player also has Corsair, each Corsair shot will periodically fire two bouncing cannonballs perpendicular to itself as it travels.; Serious Sam Publishing Contract If the player has Devolver, alongside the cannonball, Serious Cannon fires a screaming Beheaded Kamikaze who chases enemies and explodes on contact. Serious Cannon's shots will also devolve enemies.
entries.englishUsageNotes: This gun is a reference to the SBC Cannon from Serious Sam. Its item name "Seriously MENTAL" is a reference to Mental, the series' main villain.; The synergy Serious Sam Publishing Contract is a reference to Serious Sam: Kamikaze Attack!, another game published by Devolver Digital.

entries.englishGameplaySummary: This gun is unusually filthy for a handgun in the Gungeon. The Gundead spend much of their time polishing and greasing guns, though they conspicuously leave the Magnum alone.
entries.englishSynergyHighlights: Detective Magnum If the player also has 38 Special, the guns are dual-wielded.; Reload Roll If the player also has Easy Reload Bullets, dodge rolling reloads six bullets.
entries.englishUsageNotes: The Magnum is the most commonly used enemy weapon in the Gungeon, being held by Bullet Kin, Veteran Bullet Kin, Minelets, Cardinals, Shroomers, Ashen Bullet Kin, Mutant Bullet Kin, Brown, Blue, Green, and Wall Mimics, Gun Fairies, Tombstoners, Gun Cultists, Red Caped Bullet Kin, Office Bullet Kin, Bullet Fish, Candle Guys, Brollets, Kalibullets, and KBullets; The quote "5 shots or 6?" is a reference to the film Dirty Harry.

entries.unlock: Purchase from Ox and Cadence for 5 HC.
entries.englishGameplaySummary: Fires rocket propelled grenades that accelerate over a short distance.
entries.englishEffectHighlights: Fires a rocket-propelled explosive grenade. Capable of dealing considerable damage, but with a lengthy reload time.; The impact of the RPG projectile (not the explosion) deals 30% extra damage to bosses.
entries.englishSynergyHighlights: Anti-Aircraft If the player has Flak Bullets, rockets spawn seven homing flak bullets on impact.; If the player also has Roll Bomb, it will split into seven homing roll bombs when the RPG is equipped; however, they cannot actually hit enemies.
entries.englishUsageNotes: If the player feeds both this gun and Bee Hive to a Muncher, it will spit out a Stinger as a result.; 'Gundropping' (dropping a weapon mid-reload and picking it back up to force-reload it, skipping it's reload time) is highly effective with this weapon due to it's lengthy reload.

entries.unlock: Purchase from Ox and Cadence for 7 HC.
entries.englishGameplaySummary: Freezes enemies in their tracks.
entries.englishSynergyHighlights: Ice Cap If the player also has Camera or Flash Ray, Freeze Ray freezes enemies much more quickly, and the weapon's appearance also changes.; Willing To Sacrifice If the player also has Ice Cube, Heart of Ice, or Frost Bullets, Freeze Ray gains piercing and slowly regenerates ammo over time.
entries.englishUsageNotes: Has a similar look and basic abilities as the weapon used by Mr. Freeze in various "Batman" media.; This gun's flavor text is a reference to Arnold Schwarzenegger's campy performance as Mr. Freeze from the movie Batman & Robin.

entries.unlock: Purchase from Ox and Cadence for 13 HC.
entries.englishGameplaySummary: The arm of a famous bounty hunter. This arm cannon has seen substantial use, but its upkeep has been immaculate.
entries.englishSynergyHighlights: Hyper Beam If the player has Pig, when Pig dies, Heroine's ammo is fully refilled and it temporarily switches to Hyper Beam mode, where uncharged shots deal 80 damage and charged shots deal 160 damage. Heroine will automatically revert to its original form after 30 seconds. This synergy is not enabled by Lich's Eye Bullets.; y cant u crawl If the player also has Roll Bomb, its explosions are larger.
entries.englishUsageNotes: The Heroine is a reference to the Arm Cannon wielded by Samus Aran, the heavily armored bounty hunter and protagonist in the Metroid series.; Its in-game model and its alternative modes match its appearance in Metroid Prime.

entries.englishGameplaySummary: This pistol is often modified to use tranquilizer rounds. This one has not been.
entries.englishSynergyHighlights: Behold! If the player also has Com4nd0, M1911, Void Marshal, Machine Pistol, and Eye of the Beholster (the weapons the Beholster uses in game), when the Eye of the Beholster is held, the other five guns rotate around the player and shoot at nearby enemies, still using up ammo from each gun. Obtainable through the Beholster shrine, although it can be activated without.; I need scissors! 61! If the player also has Ancient Hero's Bandana, Box, Cigarettes, Ration, or Wolf, Trank Gun changes appearance, its rate of fire is decreased, damage and reload time are increased, and its bullets will inflict slow and stun. Should the player use the synergy item (such as Ration), the Trank Gun will retain the Synergy effects and appearance until using up a full magazine.
entries.englishUsageNotes: This gun is wielded by Beadies, Coalers, and the Beholster.; As it is held by the Beholster, it can be placed on the Beholster Shrine.

entries.englishGameplaySummary: The Machine Pistol is a small but powerful hand-held machine gun. It is a favorite of Bandana wearing Gundead, whose impatience with reloading is legendary.
entries.englishSynergyHighlights: Behold! If the player also has Com4nd0, M1911, Void Marshal, Eye of the Beholster, and Trank Gun (the weapons the Beholster uses in game), when the Eye of the Beholster is held, the other five guns rotate around the player and shoot at nearby enemies, still using up ammo from each gun. Obtainable through the Beholster shrine, although it can be activated without.; Pistol Machine If the player also has Fortune's Favor, Laser Sight, Nanomachines, Bionic Leg, Shock Rounds, or +1 Bullets, Machine Pistol becomes perfectly accurate, its shots are linked with electricity that can damage enemies, and its shots gain the Angry Bullets effect.
entries.englishUsageNotes: This gun resembles a Mini Uzi, a compact, blowback-operated machine pistol developed in 1980, chambered in 9x19mm Parabellum.; This gun is used by Bandana Bullet Kin, and is one of the six guns wielded by the Beholster.

entries.englishGameplaySummary: Even in death, bullets fly. This gun was crafted by Nuign the Cursed, from the skeleton of a Gundead that had become a Spectre. Nuign's body still remains somewhere deep in the Hollow, and it is from his bones that all Curse arises.
entries.englishEffectHighlights: Fires piercing, 500 skulls.
entries.englishSynergyHighlights: Dawn of the Gundead If the player also has Zombie Bullets, the chance for Skull Spitter ammo to be refunded upon missing is doubled.; Jolly Roger If the player has the VertebraeK-47, it gets the ability to freeze enemies, while Skull Spitter fires 3 skulls that are linked together.
entries.englishUsageNotes: This gun appears similar to Johnson, the talking gun from the 2011 video game Shadows of the Damned.; The description of the gun is a reference to the Jake Clover game, Nuign Spectre.

entries.englishGameplaySummary: Originally built to kill thick-skinned space mutants, the Jolter fires unreasonably large shells.
entries.englishSynergyHighlights: Heavy Jolt If the player has Heavy Bullets, Jolter gains increased knockback and shots are more accurate.; There Is Only War If the player also has Demon Head, the Demon Head will float around the player while Jolter is held, and will automatically fire at enemies when Jolter is reloaded; this won't consume the Demon Head's ammo.
entries.englishUsageNotes: The Jolter is a reference to the Warhammer 40k weapon, the Bolter, typically used by space marines. However, unlike the Bolter, the Jolter isn't an explosive weapon. The gun's sprites also reference the Bolter, as they are named Bolt_Rifle.; Having the Demon Head orbital while using the Gun That Can Kill The Past may crash the game and force you to restart the floor.

entries.englishGameplaySummary: Low rate of fire, piercing. Handcrafted by the Rifle Gundead, each Sniper Rifle performs slightly differently. Though the Gungeon rarely offers many opportunities for distance shooting, this gun still rewards a steady hand.
entries.englishSynergyHighlights: 360 Yes Scope If the player also has Scope, spinning 360 degrees before firing grants a 3-second buff that makes the next shot deal 50% more damage.; This can be activated multiple times, providing a stacking 1.5x damage multiplier for each spin. It caps at 6 rotations.
entries.englishUsageNotes: The synergy 360 Yes Scope is a reference to the 360 No Scope meme spawned by Call of Duty 4: Modern Warfare.; The Diazepam and Sniper Woof synergies are referencing Metal Gear Solid's Sniper Wolf character.

entries.englishGameplaySummary: Fires ricochet rounds. The Single Action Army may take some time to reload, but you'll hear no complaints from its users. Documents reclaimed from the Order of the True Gun imply that on occasion, a cultist briefly attains gunvana while chambering its rounds.
entries.englishSynergyHighlights: Pretty Good If the player has Box, Ration, Fortune's Favor, Nanomachines, or Hip Holster, coolness is increased by 2.; Reload Roll If the player has Easy Reload Bullets, dodge rolling reloads six bullets.
entries.englishUsageNotes: SAA refers to the Colt Single Action Army.; If combined with Angry Bullets, the shots fired from the SAA will often bounce around the room multiple times, great for fighting large, slow moving enemies.

entries.englishGameplaySummary: A regular shotgun. There's nothing special about it! Favored by the Shotgun Kin that roam the Gungeon, as it reminds them of their home.
entries.englishSynergyHighlights: Iron Stance If the player also has Heavy Boots, the gun's damage and knockback are increased by 20%, its shot speed is increased by 50%, and its reload time is decreased by 20%.; Not So Sawed-Off If the player also has Buzzkill, its saw blades are linked with lightning, the saw blades move slower, and it changes appearance.
entries.englishUsageNotes: The Regular Shotgun has an equip animation in which the gungeoneer racks the pump.; The Regular Shotgun is in the SHOTGUN gun class, meaning it benefits from the Shotgun Affinity synergy.

entries.englishGameplaySummary: Fires shattering starlight. A crossbow that shines in the moonlight of Gunymede. Charge it up, and watch the shooting stars fly.
entries.englishEffectHighlights: Fires medium-sized star projectiles. These stars split into 3 smaller, bouncing stars upon impact.; These extra stars are created at the end of the medium star's small trail, causing them to appear slightly away from the wall, enemy, or obstacle the initial star impacted.
entries.englishSynergyHighlights: Alas, Sniperion If the player also has Crown of Guns, Crown of Guns becomes Sailor Moon's tiara and shoots crescent shaped bullets that do more damage.; Land Among The Stars If the player also has Moonscraper, killing enemies with Moonscraper has a chance to send out three small Earths, which damage enemies and start orbiting the player if they fail to hit an enemy.
entries.englishUsageNotes: The Crescent Crossbow is peculiarly a part of the CHARM gun class despite not charming enemies.; This gun is a reference to the Crescent Moon Wand and Moon Scepter weapons in the Sailor Moon franchise, which use moonlight to combat evil.

entries.englishGameplaySummary: Legends tell of a gun that can kill anything with a single shot. This gun doesn't live up to the legends, but it comes close.
entries.englishSynergyHighlights: Klobbering Time If the player also has Klobbe, the Klobbe is doubled and fires two shots at a time.
entries.englishUsageNotes: It is worth noting that due to the DPS cap mechanic existent in most Bosses, shots from the AU Gun should ideally be fired 3 seconds apart.; This gun is a reference to the Golden Gun from the James Bond movie The Man with the Golden Gun. The movie's villain, the assassin Francisco Scaramanga, uses the titular Golden Gun as his weapon of choice and boasts of only needing one shot per target.

entries.englishGameplaySummary: A commonplace energy weapon. The Laser Rifle is the AK-47 of the future! Known for its low manufacturing costs and lower maintenance requirements, the Laser Rifle is used throughout the galaxy by hunters and miscreants alike.
entries.englishSynergyHighlights: Diazepam Increases damage by 50% while the Laser Rifle is held.; Laser Light Show If the player also has Scattershot, Laser Rifle becomes blue and gold, and fires 3 sets of 3 shots in a spread per trigger pull. Each shot bounces once.
entries.englishUsageNotes: The weapon is likely a reference to the Laser Rifle from X-COM: UFO Defense, which it strongly resembles in both appearance and function. In addition, the description may be a reference to the fact that the Laser Rifle could be manufactured for a low cost and sold at a profit, in addition to costing nothing but money; these factors made it an extremely ubiquitous weapon, much like the AK-47 in modern culture.; It may also be a reference to the more recent X-COM games, which also sport similar looking laser weapons.

entries.englishGameplaySummary: The favored weapon of the Void Core's frontline soldiers. Its short range ensures that any encounter will be lethal, one way or another.
entries.englishSynergyHighlights: Iron Stance If the player also has Heavy Boots, the gun's damage and knockback are increased by 20%, its shot speed is increased by 50%, and its reload time is decreased by 20%.; The Void Corps If the player has Void Core Cannon or Void Core Assault Rifle, whenever one of the guns is held, the others orbit the player. Reloading causes the orbiting gun(s) to fire towards the crosshair. This consumes no ammo from the orbiting weapons.
entries.englishUsageNotes: The Void Shotgun is in the SHOTGUN gun class, and as such is affected by the Shotgun Affinity synergy.; CQC stands for Close Quarters Combat.

entries.englishGameplaySummary: The sidearm of choice for investigative efforts.
entries.englishSynergyHighlights: Detective Magnum If the player also has Magnum, the guns are dual-wielded.; Detective Mode If the player also has Badge, the gun's maximum ammo is increased, its reload time is halved, and its damage is increased by 20%. The police officer will also show the contents of nearby chests while 38 Special is held.
entries.englishUsageNotes: Spamming the fire button while dodge rolling causes the gun to fire considerably faster for a brief time after the dodge roll has concluded.; With a fire rate of 0.07, the 38 Special requires a minimum of ~14 clicks per second to fire at maximum DPS, meaning the player is likely to be unable to take full advantage of the high fire rate.

entries.englishGameplaySummary: The Hegemony of Man has fought, and won, three major wars with alien races. Hundreds of these pistols were salvaged after the second of these wars.
entries.englishSynergyHighlights: Chief Master If the player also has any two Master Rounds, Alien Sidearm gains an increased maximum ammo of 500 and a larger magazine of 30. It becomes fully automatic, and the final two shots in the magazine become fully-charged shots.; Spartan Loadout If the player has Cat Claw, Alien Sidearm can be charged twice to fire a more powerful shot. Cat Claw pierces enemies, attaching its explosive to every enemy hit.
entries.englishUsageNotes: This gun is a reference to the alien pistols usable in Halo.; Shield Breaker is a reference to Halo's famous shield mechanic.

entries.englishGameplaySummary: Before the Void Core was disbanded by the Hegemony of Man, they were renowned throughout the sector for their feats on and off the battlefield. Following their dissolution, the famed Void Core captain known as the Thunderbolt made his way to the Gungeon.
entries.englishEffectHighlights: Fires a 3-shot burst of lasers.; Rapid-fires laser blasts.
entries.englishSynergyHighlights: Armored Corps If the player has AC-15, both guns gain increased fire rate and shot speed.; The Void Corps If the player has Void Core Cannon or Void Shotgun, whenever one of the guns are held, the others orbit the player. Reloading causes the orbiting gun(s) to fire towards the crosshair. This does not consume the ammo of the orbiting weapons.
entries.englishUsageNotes: Prior to release, this gun was originally known as the Space Core Assault Rifle with 250 ammo and 30 magazine size.; The name of the synergy "Armored Corps" is a homophone to the Armored Core game series.

entries.englishGameplaySummary: The standard issue rifle for all Hegemony of Man soldiers. Packs a decent punch, but the accuracy leaves much to be desired. Typical Hegemony work.
entries.englishEffectHighlights: Fires blue energy bolts with considerable spread.
entries.englishSynergyHighlights: Diazepam Increases damage by 50% while the Hegemony Rifle is held.; Hegemony Special Forces, Galactic Medal of Valor, or Ammo Belt Hegemony Rifle changes form, and fires accurate instantaneous piercing laser blasts.
entries.englishUsageNotes: This gun appears to be based on the DH-17 Carbine from Star Wars.; The comment about accuracy likely references the historically bad aim of Stormtroopers in Star Wars. However, Stormtroopers never used the DH-17.

entries.englishGameplaySummary: Fires a superheated beam that can ignite enemies. The Order of the True Gun tells three tales of the demons that dwell beneath the Gungeon. All end in fire.
entries.englishSynergyHighlights: Hail, Satan! Gains piercing if the player also has Pitchfork.; There Is Only War If the player also has Jolter, the Demon Head will float around the player while Jolter is held, and will automatically fire at enemies when Jolter is reloaded; this won't consume the Demon Head's ammo.
entries.englishUsageNotes: Prior to the Supply Drop Update, Demon Head did not curve towards enemies.; This gun is a reference to the Guardian Head weapon from Shadow Warrior.

entries.unlock: Reward from [[Frifle_and_the_Grey_Mauser#List_of_Quests
entries.englishGameplaySummary: Triple magic burst. Magic is unreliable in the Gungeon, unless it is based in the art of Gunjuration. Faced with low ammunition, some ingenious magic user taped several wands together in a crude approximation of a firearm.
entries.englishEffectHighlights: Fires a spread of three magic projectiles that each have a 10% chance to transmogrify enemies they hit into harmless chickens.; Bosses and Mimics are immune to being transmogrified.
entries.englishSynergyHighlights: Cast Iron If the player has Big Iron, each of Big Iron's bullets are replaced with one from Bundle of Wands, Witch Pistol, and Hexagun.; Chicken Arise If the player has Magic Bullets, the chance for Bundle of Wands to transmogrify is increased.
entries.englishUsageNotes: Despite being a shotgun, the Bundle of Wands is not in the SHOTGUN gun class, and as such does not benefit from the Shotgun Affinity synergy.; Shooting walls with the gun leaves rune-shaped indentations.

entries.englishGameplaySummary: An ancient example of fine guncraft, the Colt 1851 has been the gun of choice for many a nameless gunslinger. Skilled wielders have been known to quickly fire multiple shots, though the technique has been mostly lost to time.
entries.englishSynergyHighlights: All Out Of Law If the player has Table Tech Sight, Escape Rope, Hip Holster, or Heart Holster, Colt 1851 fires three tight spreads of three bullets upon reloading. These projectiles do not use ammo, and they can reveal secret rooms without angering the shopkeeper Bello.; Cold 1896 If the player has Cold 45, both guns' magazine sizes are doubled, and the guns are dual-wielded.
entries.englishUsageNotes: The description and pickup quote are referencing the gun's use in the film, The Good, the Bad, and the Ugly.; The name 'Blondie' used for the Colt 1851 in the Apple Arcade version of Exit the Gungeon is a reference to the nickname given to the Man With No Name in the film by the character Tuco.

entries.englishGameplaySummary: Medkits provides substantial healing when used. Contains a small piece of fairy. Seeking a place that would provide a near constant flow of the desperate and injured, Médecins Sans Diplôme recognized the Gungeon as the perfect place to found their practice.
entries.englishEffectHighlights: Heals the player for four hearts on use.; Heals the player for four hearts immediately when collected. Has no additional effect.
entries.englishSynergyHighlights: Emergency Help If the player also has Flare Gun, its reload time is decreased by 66% and its damage is increased by 50%.
entries.englishUsageNotes: Has no effect on The Robot.; If the Vampire is present on the floor, buying this item from the shop and selling the resulting health is almost always a profit. The exact margin depends on the floor but it is generally a respectable amount.

entries.englishGameplaySummary: Temporary invulnerability. A product of Professor Goopton's rigorous research.
entries.englishEffectHighlights: While active, the player turns gray, becoming invincible and reflecting bullets back at enemies.; Reflected bullets deal 10 damage.
entries.englishSynergyHighlights: Five O'Clock Somewhere If the player also has Crestfaller, reloading it will fire a glass bottle that has a chance to stun enemies hit. Reloading Crestfaller while standing on a liquid replenishes the ammo missing in the current magazine, similar to Gungine.; Terminated, Too If the player has Grenade Launcher, the grenades fire 8 bullets in all directions upon exploding that set enemies on fire.
entries.englishUsageNotes: This item is a reference to the classic Potion of Stoneskin from Dungeons and Dragons.; Both the tagline and the item's effect could be references to Metal Gear Rising's Sundowner, a boss who employs a shield to deflect the player's attacks while boasting of his invincibility.

entries.englishGameplaySummary: The Knife Shield is a shield made of blasphemous knives. Be wary of using it, as it is believed to bring upon the anger of the Jammed.
entries.englishEffectHighlights: Creates a ring of 8 knives around the player that damage enemies and block enemy bullets. Knives vanish after taking enough damage.; If used again before Knife Shield is recharged, the knives are launched forward.
entries.englishSynergyHighlights: Fightknives If the player has Fightsabre, activating Knife Shield while holding Fightsabre makes the knives reflect enemy bullets before getting destroyed. This synergy effect remains even if you switch weapons (provided you were holding Fightsabre upon activation).; Running Blades If the player has Deck4rd, killing an enemy with Deck4rd has a high chance to spawn a knife.
entries.englishUsageNotes: The knives do not carry on between floors, and disappear once the player enters a new floor.; Knife Shield can be activated while the player already has knives, adding another 8 knives to the ring.

entries.englishGameplaySummary: This mine activates when an enemy gets close.
entries.englishEffectHighlights: Places a mine that explodes when an enemy comes near it, dealing 60 damage.
entries.englishUsageNotes: The mine does not seem to explode upon contact with mimics, even when already revealed.; This item is a reference to the Proximity Mine in GoldenEye 007.

entries.englishGameplaySummary: This clock holds mystical properties that enable the activator to slow down time.
entries.englishEffectHighlights: Slows down time by 70% while active. The player's movement, firing and reload speeds are unaffected by the slowdown.
entries.englishUsageNotes: Bluer Guon Stone If the player also has Blue Guon Stone, the size and rotation speed of the Guon Stone is increased and it rotates at a fixed distance from the player while they are moving instead of on a slight delay. If the Guon Stone blocks a bullet, it deals 15 damage to all nearby enemies.; Thrown For A Bloop If the player also has Blooper, while Bullet Time is active, a Blooper orbits the player and continually fires at enemies.

entries.englishGameplaySummary: Distracts enemies. Most inhabitants of the Gungeon are not the most intelligent of creatures, and will fire upon anything that they do not recognize. This crude fascimile of a Gungeoneer will hold their attention for a time. Many Gundead seem content to lock trespassers up in cells, despite their trigger-happy natures. Somewhere deep in the Gungeon, there is probably a prison full of Decoys.
entries.englishEffectHighlights: Places a decoy at the users current position. This decoy will distract enemies, who will target the decoy instead of the player. This decoy will be destroyed after taking too much damage.; If a decoy is placed in a Shop, it will also distract the shopkeeper, allowing the player to steal from that shop while the Decoy is inside.
entries.englishSynergyHighlights: Deadly Distraction If the player also has Monster Blood, the Decoy creates a large poison pool when attacked.; Decoy Octorok If the player also has Abyssal Tentacle, the decoy will become a charmed enemy once destroyed.
entries.englishUsageNotes: Items cannot be purchased from a shop with a decoy in it; stealing is the only option. Place a decoy close to the shop's entrance and shoot it from outside the shop to enable buying items normally again.; The item itself is a reference to the Body Replacement Technique from Naruto, in which the user replaces themselves with a log to avoid an opponents attack.

entries.englishGameplaySummary: Deflects bullets for a few seconds, similar to Potion of Lead Skin.
entries.englishEffectHighlights: Deflects bullets for a few seconds, similar to Potion of Lead Skin.; Recharges once every two rooms cleared.

entries.englishGameplaySummary: Calls in an ammo drop.
entries.englishEffectHighlights: Upon use, calls in a crate containing Ammo at the players position.; Cannot be used if the player is holding a gun with full ammo.
entries.englishUsageNotes: Supply Drops have a very low chance to drop on room clear.; Players can stack multiple uses if they acquire more than one Supply Drop.

entries.englishGameplaySummary: The Makarov was brought to the Gungeon by a lost Cosmonaut. Some say that the brave, lost soul still wanders the Gungeon. This gun is no better or worse than any other gun.
entries.englishSynergyHighlights: Mak Pak If the player has Bottle or Backpack, Makarov's maximum ammo is increased by 30%. While holding Marakov, it occasionally fires a homing bullet at an enemy for free.; No Odd Job If the player has Klobbe, the two guns are dual-wielded. Their damage is increased by 20% and spread is increased by 60%, decreasing accuracy.
entries.englishUsageNotes: This gun is based on the real life Russian firearm Pistolet Makarova (Makarov's Pistol).; This gun's description references The Cosmonaut, who was originally going to be found in the game as a Gungeoneer, but got cut during development. The gun may also reference the fact that before going into space Russian astronauts are given a gun.

entries.englishGameplaySummary: This Budget Revolver was brought to the Gungeon by an infamous fugitive. Provided by the Hegemony Regional Magistrate. The Convict won their plea to face the Gungeon in lieu of life imprisonment; undo their crimes, or face an eternity in the Gungeon. With no cost to the state, it was an acceptable arrangement. Cheaply made and prone to jams, the Budget Revolver is used by only the most desperate Gungeoneers.
entries.englishUsageNotes: Cannot be dropped.; Cannot be obtained by any Gungeoneer other than The Convict or The Paradox.

entries.englishGameplaySummary: Fires massive explosive rounds. Following the tragic Ten Days' War, a majority of the Hegemony's Shock Troops were decommissioned. A small number of the combat robots escaped however, and they were pursued relentlessly by special forces. This explosive hand cannon belonged to one such detective who tracked his prey to the Gungeon.
entries.englishUsageNotes: Although classified as semi-automatic, the Deck4rd is functionally automatic, as its rate of fire does not benefit from tapping the fire button instead of holding it down.; Blade Runner If the player has Origuni, magazine size of both guns is increased by 50%.

entries.unlock: Reward from [[Frifle_and_the_Grey_Mauser#List_of_Quests
entries.englishGameplaySummary: An inelegant weapon for a less civilized age. These guns were once used to make pianos out of teeth.
entries.englishEffectHighlights: Fires a conical spread of bullets with strong knockback.
entries.englishSynergyHighlights: Iron Stance If the player also has Heavy Boots, the gun's damage and knockback are increased by 20%, its shot speed is increased by 50%, and its reload time is decreased by 20%.; The Elephant In The Room If the player also has Pig, Turkey, Owl, Chicken Flute, Super Space Turtle, Bait Launcher, or Compressed Air Tank the Elephant Gun becomes an elephant head and shoots a pink elephant instead of bullets. The elephant deals 30 damage and has strong knockback, but decreased fire rate.
entries.englishUsageNotes: So called elephant guns were large guns designed for the hunting of large animals (such as elephants) during safaris in Africa in the 19th century. Real-world elephant guns fired single large slugs, rather than shot.; The Ammonomicon description "These guns were once used to make pianos out of teeth." is a reference to the practice of hunting elephants to harvest their tusks for ivory, a material once used to make piano keys.

entries.englishGameplaySummary: This gun is still in the process of being forged! It barely functions in its current state, but could be a viable choice in a truly desperate situation. A small hourglass is embossed on the cylinder.
entries.englishSynergyHighlights: Phoenix Up If the player has Phoenix, while Phoenix is held, summons a phoenix companion that fires at enemies.
entries.englishUsageNotes: Despite not actually igniting enemies, the Unfinished Gun is in the FIRE gun class.; As a consequence, having the Unfinished Gun in the player's inventory makes them less likely to find other guns in the FIRE class that actually burn enemies.

entries.englishGameplaySummary: Favored by the huge predatory bird that nests in the Gungeon, it is a weapon to be feared.
entries.englishSynergyHighlights: Heavy Artillery If the player also has Com4nd0, both weapons are dual-wielded.; Not Quite As Mini If the player has Ammo Belt, Drum Clip, or Oiled Cylinder, the Vulcan Cannon changes its appearance, its maximum ammo is increased by 100, and its fire rate gradually increases as long as the trigger is held.
entries.englishUsageNotes: According to its ammonomicon entry, the Vulcan Cannon is wielded by the Gatling Gull, however its visual appearance is starkly different to the minigun the Gatling Gull can be seen holding in battle.; The gun is a reference to a common trope of action films where people will carry handheld M134 Miniguns, which in reality is very difficult and impractical.

stats.value: 14.5 (15.6 with The Marine's starting Military Training)
1.2s (0.96 with The Marine's starting Military Training)
5 (4.15 with The Marine's starting Military Training)

entries.englishGameplaySummary: The Marine Sidearm was brought to the Gungeon by a low-ranking Primerdyne soldier. Though this gun appears sturdy, it has been known to fail when it is most needed.
entries.englishUsageNotes: Cannot be dropped.; Is technically automatic, as tapping the fire button does not provide a faster fire rate than holding it down.

entries.unlock: Purchase from Ox and Cadence for 2 HC.
entries.englishGameplaySummary: This archaic laser has been studied for centuries. Gungeonologists can only conclude that it was used by primitive people to poison each other with radiation.
entries.englishEffectHighlights: Fires a green laser beam that slightly curves towards enemies.; Continually shooting an enemy with the laser for 0.25 seconds will poison them for 3.5 seconds.
entries.englishSynergyHighlights: Beta Ray If the player also has Irradiated Lead, Gamma Ray's beam grows larger and deals 23 damage per second, but no longer travels instantaneously.; Gungeonite If the player also has Clear Guon Stone, Gamma Ray will leave a trail of poison.
entries.englishUsageNotes: Prior to the Supply Drop Update, Gamma Ray did not curve towards enemies.; The Ammonomicon quote "Mean Green" may be a reference to The Hulk, who was transformed by exposure to gamma radiation.

stats.value: 7 (3.5 with The Robot's starting Battery Bullets)

entries.englishGameplaySummary: The right hand of an assault robot that fled to the Gungeon. When weilded by the Robot, combines with Junk to increase all damage output. It is a simple projectile delivery system. Reliable, but its short barrel is lacking in intricacy. Under the control of a targeting AI it becomes fearsome indeed.
entries.englishUsageNotes: Cannot be dropped.; Cannot be obtained by any Gungeoneer other than The Robot or The Paradox.

entries.englishGameplaySummary: The Rogue Special, given to the Pilot by his partner. It's never let him down. This hip shooter has been known to fire half-cocked and often gets itself into more trouble than it can handle.
entries.englishUsageNotes: Cannot be dropped.; Cannot be obtained by any Gungeoneer other than The Pilot or The Paradox.

entries.englishGameplaySummary: The reproductive gun of the terrible Beholster. When wielded by its creator, it summons young Beholster spawn from the void.
entries.englishEffectHighlights: Fires instant laser projectiles.; The final shot of each cylinder fires a projectile shaped like a pink Beadie. This beadie-projectile moves slowly forwards, periodically firing more bullets in the direction of the nearest enemy.
entries.englishSynergyHighlights: Behold! If the player also has Com4nd0, M1911, Void Marshal, Machine Pistol, and Trank Gun (the weapons the Beholster uses in game), increases damage by 30%, magazine size by 50%, spread by 50%, decreases reload time by 33%, and slows down enemy bullets by 20% when one of the guns is held. When the Eye of the Beholster is held, the other five guns rotate around the player and shoot at nearby enemies, still using up ammo from each gun randomly. Obtainable through the Beholster shrine, although it can be activated without.; This synergy can also be activated if the player has 5 of these weapons and Lich's Eye Bullets.
entries.englishUsageNotes: The Beholster wields this gun.; As it is held by the Beholster, it can be placed on the Beholster Shrine.

entries.englishGameplaySummary: Originally crafted by a gunsmith who favored revolvers but was envious of of the automatic weaponry being developed by his peers. It seems like he accidentally left one of his tools in the clip...?
entries.englishEffectHighlights: Rapid-fires a barrage of bullets.; The last shot in every magazine is a high-damage hammer.
entries.englishSynergyHighlights: Hammer and Nail If the player also has Nail Gun, maximum ammo is increased by 50% for both guns. Nail Gun deals triple damage, while H4mmer gets 5 hammer shots at the end of every magazine and spins a wood beam around the player when reloading an empty magazine.
entries.englishUsageNotes: This gun may be a reference to the trick of "fanning the hammer", where a single-action revolver is fired by flicking the hammer of the weapon rather than pulling the trigger in order to make it shoot faster.; Bug: There is an issue with the Hammer and Nail synergy where if the ammo count exceeds what the base gun would have without the 50% maximum ammo, the current ammo count would revert to that amount. Additionally, partial ammo kits cannot have the ammo go above the base gun's amount, unless it is applied to H4mmer itself.

entries.englishGameplaySummary: Originally used to swat down bothersome aircraft, the Stinger has become a favorite of the aim deficient Gungeoneer.
entries.englishEffectHighlights: Fires an explosive missile, and three 150 bees that seek out and attack enemies. Bees deal 3 damage on impact and apply a lingering 'swarm' effect that deals 1 damage right away, and deals 1 damage every second for 2 more seconds.; When the missile impacts an enemy or obstacle, it will create 5 additional bees.
entries.englishSynergyHighlights: Particle Flow If the player also has Laser Sight, a crosshair will appear on any enemy the Stinger points towards and its homing rockets will be guided to them.
entries.englishUsageNotes: Giving the Bee Hive and RPG to the Muncher will always result in a Stinger.; The Stingers long reload time makes it especially affected by the 'gun-dropping' technique. Dropped guns are instantly reloaded, and with guns like the Stinger the process of dropping them and quickly picking them back up is faster than reloading normally.

entries.englishGameplaySummary: A masterwork shotgun. The right answer isn't always a gun that shoots bees, a water gun, or a flaming hand. Sometimes, all you need is a simple concept executed immaculately. This gold trimmed, double-barreled shotgun represents the best of traditional guncraft.
entries.englishEffectHighlights: Fires a tight spread of shotgun pellets that deal significant damage, but have short range.; Increases Coolness by 1.
entries.englishSynergyHighlights: The Ecstasy Of Gold If the player also has Gilded Bullets, Old Goldie's damage is increased by 25%.; Iron Stance If the player also has Heavy Boots, the gun's damage and knockback are increased by 20%, its shot speed is increased by 50%, and its reload time is decreased by 20%.
entries.englishUsageNotes: When a gun is Duct Taped to Old Goldie, it too will receive the 70% damage boost. However, when Old Goldie is duct taped to another gun, the damage boost is lost. Be sure to use Old Goldie as the base weapon to receive the boost.; Old Goldie is in the SHOTGUN gun class, meaning it benefits from the Shotgun Affinity synergy.

entries.englishGameplaySummary: High rate of fire and relatively compact, making it a favorite among Gungeoneers and criminals alike.
entries.englishSynergyHighlights: Iroquois If the player also has Eyepatch, Rolling Eye, or Cigarettes, the MAC10's reload time is decreased by 33%, magazine size is increased by 50%, and knockback is increased by 50%.; The Submachinist If the player also has Machine Pistol, both weapons are dual wielded and are fired simultaneously.
entries.englishUsageNotes: The item's subtext is a reference to the Biggie Smalls song 'Come On'.; The synergy with the Eyepatch is a reference to Kurt Russell as Snake Plissken in the 1981 movie Escape from New York. Snake wears an eyepatch over his left eye and primarily uses a MAC10. 'Iroquois Plissken' is also an alias used by Snake in the Metal Gear series. The character of Snake is heavily based on Snake Plissken, including wearing an eyepatch.

entries.unlock: Speak to the Tinker after completing the [[Shortcuts
entries.englishGameplaySummary: Can unlock chests. The AKEY-47 is a masterpiece of Gungeoncraft, perfectly combining the masterful gunplay of an AK-47 with the incredibly useful ability to open locks. An inscription along the frame reads, "EX ARCHA, LIBERTAS."
entries.englishSynergyHighlights: Akey Breaky If the player has the Shelleton Key, AKEY-47 fires skulls that do 10 damage per skull, receives infinite ammo, and changes in appearance to resemble a Shelleton. Its shots will no longer open locks or reveal secret rooms, though chests can still be opened due to possessing the Shelleton Key.; If the player has obtained this synergy via Lich's Eye Bullets, opening a chest or door with the AKEY-47 equipped will still unlock it for free, if the player has no keys.
entries.englishUsageNotes: The AKEY-47 Has identical statistics to the AK-47.; Once unlocked, the AKEY-47 may appear for sale in Flynts shop for 3 keys.

entries.unlock: Purchase from Trorc for 5 HC.
entries.englishGameplaySummary: Alternates with grenades. The M16 is a classic military-issue machine gun that upon reload, switches projectiles to grenade launcher.
entries.englishEffectHighlights: Reloading the M16 alternates between a burst-fire mode which shoots a burst of three bullets with every trigger pull, and a grenade launcher mode which lobs explosive projectiles.; Grenades are affected by gravity, falling quickly towards the bottom of the screen.
entries.englishSynergyHighlights: Assault and Battery If the player also has Shock Rifle, the guns are dual wielded.; Keep The Change If the player has Blank Companion's Ring, it will occasionally trigger a blank upon shooting the last bullet in a magazine of the M16.
entries.englishUsageNotes: Grenades consume 1 ammo each, the exact same as a regular bullet. As such, reloading immediately when in machine gun mode allows the gun to spend most of its time in grenade launcher mode at no additional ammo cost. This can be desirable for an increased dps, and the ability to hit multiple enemies at once with each explosion.; Allowing the M16 to reload passively by switching to another weapon for a few seconds does not switch the gun's form.

entries.unlock: Complete the Hollow 20 times.
entries.englishGameplaySummary: A mysterious gun that grows in strength on each kill. Substantial power is lost any time its wielder is damaged. Stories are told of a rebellious android that once used this gun in a conflict deep underground. The handle is marked with two small tick marks.
entries.englishEffectHighlights: Has three different levels, with the gun leveling up after enough enemies are slain. Higher levels deal more damage.; Killing 11 enemies will level up Polaris to level 2.
entries.englishSynergyHighlights: Star Friends If the player also has Stuffed Star, while Stuffed Star is active and Polaris is held, the player continually fires maximum level Polaris shots in all directions.; Square Brace If the player also has Bracket Key or Bionic Leg, Polaris gains 20% increased damage, increased ammo capacity of 700, increased magazine size of 30, increased fire rate when leveled up, automatic firing, and a new appearance.
entries.englishUsageNotes: Polaris' level does not affect its selling price.; While level 1 and 3 fire a single projectile of varying damage, level 2 fires two. Because of this, level 2 projectiles have double the probability of gaining chance-based bullet effects (e.g., Vorpal Bullets, Katana Bullets, Hungry Bullets, etc.).

entries.unlock: Purchase from Trorc for 12 HC.
entries.englishGameplaySummary: A powerful machine gun that once belonged to a forgotten hero. This gun's mechanism is overly complicated, but it proves extremely effective in the field. Beloved by many, despite its flaws.
entries.englishEffectHighlights: Fires high-accuracy bullets that can pierce up to 3 times.; Rate of fire increases the longer the trigger is held.
entries.englishSynergyHighlights: Gunderlord If the player has Ice Breaker, Patriot shots are connected with electricity. Ice Breaker fires single high-velocity shots, and its ammo slowly regenerates over time.; What A Thrill If the player has Ration, Cigarettes, Fortune's Favor, Ancient Hero's Bandana, or Box, Patriot has a chance to transmogrify enemies into snakes.
entries.englishUsageNotes: This gun is a reference to the weapon of the same name in Metal Gear Solid 3: Snake Eater.; The Ammonomicon quote "Gun Eater" is a reference to the title of the game- "Snake Eater".

entries.englishGameplaySummary: The Rusty Sidearm was brought to the Gungeon by the Hunter. Despite its extremely dated design and some signs of rust, this gun appears relatively new. Very strange...
entries.englishUsageNotes: Cannot be dropped.; Cannot be obtained by any Gungeoneer other than The Hunter or The Paradox.

entries.unlock: Reward from [[Frifle and the Grey Mauser#List of Quests
entries.englishGameplaySummary: This magic horn is proof that at least one unicorn existed, and now is either dead or hornless.
entries.englishSynergyHighlights: Crystal Horn If the player has Luxin Cannon, Luxin Cannon gets three crystal shots at the end of every magazine, and the crystal bursts into many more crystals. Additionally, when an enemy is killed with the Unicorn Horn, they explode into Luxin Cannon projectiles.; Double Rainbow If the player has Stuffed Star, while Stuffed Star is active, five Unicorn Horn beams are fired from the player in all directions.
entries.englishUsageNotes: The song played by the Unicorn Horn is Unicorn Power!, composed by Enter the Gungeon sound designer Erica Hampson.; If the player has Helix Bullets, the 2 rainbows fired will follow the same path, and will not lose their homing property, unlike most other homing guns with Helix Bullets.

entries.englishGameplaySummary: A standard scope. Increases accuracy!
entries.englishEffectHighlights: Reduces shot spread by 60%, increasing accuracy.
entries.englishUsageNotes: 360 Yes Scope While holding A.W.P. or Sniper Rifle, spinning 360 degrees before firing makes the shot deal 50% more damage.; The buff can stack up to 6 times, and each instance of the buff will last for 3 seconds, providing an additional 1.5x damage multiplier for each instance.

entries.unlock: Purchase from Trorc for 7 HC.
entries.englishGameplaySummary: Provides healing on use. If equipped, will be used automatically on death. This MRE comes in the form of a dry and dense cookie. It doesn't taste great, but it delivers the calories the body needs.
entries.englishEffectHighlights: Heals the player for two full hearts.; Automatically used if the player runs out of health.
entries.englishSynergyHighlights: I need scissors! 61! If the player also has Trank Gun, Trank Gun changes appearance, its rate of fire is decreased, damage and reload time are increased, and its bullets will inflict slow and stun. Should the player use the synergy item (such as Ration), the Trank Gun will retain the Synergy effects and appearance until using up a full magazine.; Pretty Good If the player has SAA, it increases coolness by 2 and accuracy by 25% while it's held.
entries.englishUsageNotes: Has no effect for The Robot.; Prior to the Supply Drop Update, Ration was an A tier item.

entries.englishGameplaySummary: Enemy bullets go wide. An experimental device worn by a particularly lucky Gungeoneer. In the end, it wasn't enough to save her life, but it certainly extended it.
entries.englishEffectHighlights: While active, enemy bullets are repulsed from the player- curving around them.
entries.englishSynergyHighlights: Dead Cell If player has Railgun, its charge time is decreased by 10% and its damage to bosses is increased by 30%.; Pistol Machine If the player has Machine Pistol, its shots pierce enemies and are connected with electricity.
entries.englishUsageNotes: The curving effect is not powerful enough to redirect bullets with significant velocity (e.g. Sniper Shell projectiles) or bigger bullets (e.g. Cannonbalrog's disappearing attack).; The synergy "Pretty Good" is a reference to the popular quote from Metal Gear Solid, "You're pretty good".

entries.englishGameplaySummary: Grants flight, but restricts the dodge roll. Only a Gungeoneer with a deathwish would give up their dodge roll. Luckily, this jetpack can easily be toggled on or off.
entries.englishEffectHighlights: Use to toggle the jetpack on and off. While active, the players Movement Speed is increased by 2.5 and the player gains flight, but cannot dodge roll.; Passing over pools of flammable goop such as oil or Phaser Spider webs while the jetpack is active will set them on fire.
entries.englishSynergyHighlights: Barrage Shot If the player also has Charge Shot, charge time is reduced by 25%, reload time is increased by 50%, and shot size is increased.; MM6 Mini Rocket If the player has Backpack, activating the Jetpack fires a rocket in the direction of the crosshair. This has a cooldown of about 5 seconds.
entries.englishUsageNotes: The Barrage Shot synergy is a reference to the game Charge Shot, which the weapon of the same name in Enter the Gungeon is a reference to.
