Combatants in Charge Shot use jetpacks for mobility.; Bug: If Jetpack is active when entering the Resourceful Rat's Lair, the loading screen will be the default loading screen instead of the rat variant.

entries.unlock: Purchase from Ox and Cadence for 8 HC.
entries.englishGameplaySummary: Damages all enemies on screen, no matter what. This cannon was built for mounting on Supersonic Attack Fighters, but has been retrofitted to be carried by infantry. An inscription on the barrel reads, "Know thy own position, and all others inevitably fall."
entries.englishSynergyHighlights: RAIDEN If the player also has Space Friend, maximum ammo is increased by 25%, and 2 homing bullets shoot out periodically from the sides of the weapon. It also drastically reduces its ammo consumption.
entries.englishUsageNotes: Like other guns, this will not fire if the player is up against a wall and trying to shoot into the wall, even though this gun shoots through walls.; Backup Gun indirectly doubles this gun's damage due to its direct homing.

entries.englishGameplaySummary: Explodes after a short delay. Countless experienced adventurers have brought Bombs to the Gungeon seeking secret doors, only to be foiled by the existence of Blanks. Still, explosives have their place.
entries.englishEffectHighlights: Throws a bomb onto the ground that explodes after a short delay, dealing 60 damage to nearby enemies.; Triggers an explosion at the position of every enemy on screen.
entries.englishSynergyHighlights: Bomberpal If the player has Lil' Bomber, it fires two bombs instead of one, charges twice as fast, and has 40% lower reload time.; Fairy Bow If the player also has Bow, its charge time is halved and the Bow has a chance to fire an extra arrow, similar to the Shadow Bullets effect.
entries.englishUsageNotes: The bomb's explosion can open secret rooms.; Bomb can be used to hurt Cannonbalrog while it is performing its invulnerable attack.

entries.englishGameplaySummary: Freezes enemies.
entries.englishEffectHighlights: Throws a bomb that deals 8 damage and freezes enemies.
entries.englishUsageNotes: Smart Bombs If the player has Homing Bullets or Crutch, Ice Bomb will home in on enemies.; Battle Mode If the player has Orbital Bullets, creates three bombs which orbit around the player, blocking bullets and exploding in contact with enemies. These bombs won't deal damage to the player, but will knock them back, which could send them into a hazard.

entries.englishGameplaySummary: Improves health, movement speed, and damage. This treat was originally created as a reward for Winchester's games of skill.
entries.englishEffectHighlights: Adds 1 heart container.; Increases by 1.5.
entries.englishUsageNotes: Sweetness and Light If the player also has Magic Lamp, it fires three projectiles at a time. The shots deal 25% of their original damage, but the genie deals full damage.; Like other items that grant heart containers, this item gives The Robot 7 to 10 coin upon pickup.

entries.englishGameplaySummary: Increases damage at the cost of bullet velocity. These faceted bullets glitter with colors unseen in the depths of the Gungeon.
entries.englishEffectHighlights: Increases damage by 25%.; Halves bullet speed.
entries.englishSynergyHighlights: Alternative Rock If the player has Face Melter, instead of firing four streams, Face Melter fires two parallel columns of notes. Reloading fires a guitar pick with a chance to stun.; Heavy Jolt If the player has Jolter, it gains increased knockback.
entries.englishUsageNotes: Thrown guns will increase in size, and will remain large even after picking them back up. This is purely a cosmetic effect and has no effect on gameplay.; Due to the higher knockback and slower shot speed, this item can be detrimental with short-ranged guns.

entries.englishGameplaySummary: Sometimes reveals the floor. The Gungeon is unmappable, but it was not always so. It is said that in his youth, the great cartographer Woban has created four great maps, one for each floor of the Gungeon. While working on the fifth and final map, the walls suddenly began to shift strangely; they continue to do so to this day.
entries.englishEffectHighlights: New floors have a 50% chance to be mapped out.; Does not reveal secret rooms.
entries.englishUsageNotes: Cartographer's Ring does not reveal the trapdoor to the Resourceful Rat's Lair.

entries.unlock: Purchase from Ox and Cadence for 4 HC.
entries.englishGameplaySummary: Increased bullet speed and power. Known for her impatience, Cadence grew tired of waiting for her bullets to reach their target. She developed these tiny rockets to give each shot extra speed.
entries.englishEffectHighlights: Increases the shot speed of the player's bullets by 50%.; Increases damage by 10%.
entries.englishSynergyHighlights: Shot Across The Bow If the player also has Gunbow, it fires explosive missiles.
entries.englishUsageNotes: The active item exclusive to The Pilot's past that fires a volley of rockets shares an identical sprite with Rocket-Powered Bullets.

entries.englishGameplaySummary: Movement speed increased.
entries.englishEffectHighlights: Increases Movement Speed by 1.5.; Grants a piece of Armor.
entries.englishSynergyHighlights: Brave New World If the player also has Siren, while it is equipped, a fish companion follows the player that can block bullets and shows contents of chests without opening them.; If the player has revealed the contents of a chest, the contents of that chest will be permanently locked in as that item. If a chest with a locked-in item attempts to drop a random item when broken, it will always drop the item that was locked in, overriding the usual quality reduction.
entries.englishUsageNotes: The quote upon picking up this item is a reference to a Star Wars: Episode VI – Return of the Jedi quote. Obi-Wan talks about Anakin's fall into the Dark Side saying "he's more machine now than man. Twisted and evil."; The synergy Brave New World is a reference to the Disney movie, "The Little Mermaid".

entries.englishGameplaySummary: This ballot is proof of participation in the democratic process. PSA: Voting is cool, no matter what anyone says!
entries.englishEffectHighlights: Increases Coolness by 3, which decreases the cooldown of active items and increases the chance of items dropping upon clearing a room.
entries.englishSynergyHighlights: Paperwork If the player also has Origuni, the first round of each magazine fires three airplanes instead of one.; Revolution If the player also has Dueling Pistol, the Dueling Pistol's bullets will deal 25% more damage and bounce five times instead of twice.
entries.englishUsageNotes: If duplicates of this item are somehow obtained, further increases Coolness.; This item being in a gun-themed game is likely a reference to the Abraham Lincoln quote "The ballot is stronger than the bullet."

entries.englishGameplaySummary: Repurposes spent ammo from fallen Gundead.
entries.englishEffectHighlights: Adds a 10% chance to regain 5% of the currently held gun's maximum ammo (rounded down) upon killing an enemy.
entries.englishSynergyHighlights: Extreme Operation If the player also has Melted Rock, Melted Rock causes each corpse to fire a bullet at a nearby enemy in addition to exploding.; Synthetic Shield If the player also has Heart Synthesizer or Armor Synthesizer, grants a familiar that orbits the player and blocks shots. If all three synthesizers are picked up, grants three familiars.
entries.englishUsageNotes: If 5% of a gun's maximum ammo is less than 1 (less than 20 maximum ammo), Ammo Synthesizer will restore 1 ammo when it activates.; Works well with Ancient Hero's Bandana as it will restore 4x more ammo effectively making it easier to not run out.

entries.unlock: Purchase from Ox and Cadence for 8 HC.
entries.englishGameplaySummary: Increases damage at the cost of accuracy. The runes carved into the leather of this eyepatch represent a bargain with Kaliber, a revered gun Goddess. In exchange for depth perception, she will bless each bullet fired.
entries.englishEffectHighlights: Increases damage by 35%.; Increases shot spread by 65%, making shots less accurate.
entries.englishSynergyHighlights: Careful Iteration If the player has Casey, Casey becomes spiked and fires a spread of 6 nails each time it is swung.; Iroquois If the player also has MAC10, its reload time is decreased by 33%, its magazine size is increased by 50%, and its knockback is increased by 50%.
entries.englishUsageNotes: This item is a reference to the Norse god Odin, in particular the poem Völuspá of the Poetic Edda, where it is explained that Odin cast his eye into the well 'Mímisbrunnr' in exchange for wisdom. The runes supposedly carved into the eyepatch item are likely in reference to the nordic Elder Futhark, the oldest runic script.; The Iroquois synergy is a reference to the 1981 film Escape from New York, where the protagonist Snake Plissken wears an eyepatch over his left eye and carries a MAC10.

entries.englishGameplaySummary: Improves equipped gun on each sequential kill. Taking damage or changing guns will reduce the bonus back to zero.
entries.englishEffectHighlights: Grants a +2% damage bonus for each enemy killed. The effect maxes out at +150% weapon damage (75 consecutive kills).; The bonus is reset if the player takes damage, changes guns, or drops their gun.
entries.englishSynergyHighlights: Keeping The Beat If the player has Drum Clip, the damage increase is changed to +4% damage per kill instead of +2%, and maxes out at +250% damage instead of +150%. The note at max level becomes silver. Picking up Drum Clip after Metronome resets the damage bonus.; Shredder Doubles the spread of the Face Melter and Gunzheng.
entries.englishUsageNotes: Adding currently equipped guns to a Muncher will reset the bonus as if switching guns.; Playing Winchester's minigame will reset the bonus.

entries.englishGameplaySummary: Fires a beam that decoheres matter into its constituent particles. This disintegration ray is outlawed on almost every planet with some form of government, and feared by all beings made primarily of matter. It can be fired legally in only two places: the Gungeon, and the waste disposal facilities of Popolon V.
entries.englishSynergyHighlights: Weird Science If the player also has Science Cannon, three pairs of Science Cannon beams branch off perpendicular to the Disintegrator's main beam.; If the player also has Singularity, shooting an upgraded Disintegrator beam at or near the black hole will cause a massive number of beams to split off in every direction.
entries.englishUsageNotes: The Disintegrator appears to be a reference to the "M-920 Cain" heavy weapon from Mass Effect 2, having a similar design and very long charge time before firing.https://www.youtube.com/watch?v=BF_iYmF_tE8 video of the M-920 Cain being fired at powerful enemies

entries.englishGameplaySummary: Used mostly by farmers who seek to defend their farms from hungry giants, the Blunderbuss fires anything you put into it. Hold to charge and launch projectiles in an inaccurate short spray. Not as effective against sly dragons.
entries.englishSynergyHighlights: Blunderbrace If the player also has Macho Brace, Blunderbuss shoots bigger and faster bullets and charge time is reduced.; Hell Singing If the player also has Silver Bullets, Blunderbuss will deal an extra 50% damage to jammed enemies for a total of 487.5% damage.
entries.englishUsageNotes: Despite firing like a shotgun, the Blunderbuss is not in the SHOTGUN gun class, and as such does not benefit from the Shotgun Affinity synergy.; There is a glitch where firing Blunderbuss by charging until just before the fully charged animation causes the gun to fire without using ammo.

entries.englishGameplaySummary: Fires several pulses of decoherent energy. An early, less controlled version of modern energy weaponry.
entries.englishSynergyHighlights: Finger on the Pulse If the player has Heart Bottle, Heart Holster, Heart Purse, Heart Locket, or Heart Lunchbox and takes damage while holding Pulse Cannon, three miniature Pulse Cannons spawn around the player and fire at enemies, before disappearing. This effect stacks with itself, each adding 3 more pulse cannons for each heart item.; Iron Stance If the player also has Heavy Boots, projectile speed and knockback are increased.
entries.englishUsageNotes: This weapon may be a reference to the M41A Pulse Rifle in the film Aliens.; The entry for the Pulse Cannon in the Physical Ammonomicon has a small doodle of a monstrous Bullet Kin with another Bullet Kin head emerging out of its toothy maw. This is likely a reference to the iconic inner jaw of Xenomorphs in the Alien franchise.

entries.englishGameplaySummary: Low damage, high rate of fire. Spiny! Remnants of Gun Cacti can be found throughout the Gungeon, but no one is certain as to their source. Perhaps they hail from a yet undiscovered Chamber? Regardless, their impressive needle capacity is a boon to any Gungeoneer.
entries.englishSynergyHighlights: Cactus Flower If the player has Broccoli or Orange, Cactus has a chance to fire a prickly pear that bounces off walls and fires needles in all directions.; The chance to fire a prickly pear also applies to guns Duct Taped to Cactus with the synergy.
entries.englishUsageNotes: "1000 Needles!" is a reference to a move used by the Cactuar, a recurring enemy from the Final Fantasy series, who resembles a walking cactus.; "Succulent DOOM" may possibly be a reference to the album Special Herbs by MF DOOM.

entries.englishGameplaySummary: Sets enemies aflame. Alben Smallbore, enthralled with the variety of guns he found in the Gungeon, created this spell. Upon casting, the wielder's hand bursts into flame, allowing fireballs to be thrown like bullets.
entries.englishSynergyHighlights: Hotter Than Heck If the player has Pitchfork, increases the shot size and damage of the guns by 25%, and grants fire immunity while they are held.; Maximize Spell If the player also has Chance Bullets or Gungeon Pepper, the Flame Hand turns green, is always guaranteed to roll at least a 12 (after intelligence modifiers), and fires green flames that no longer pierce enemies but leave pools of green napalm upon hitting enemies. Green napalm does not damage the player, and inflicts greenfire on enemies, which has a massively increased duration.
entries.englishUsageNotes: As the Flame Hand deals fire-type damage, it deals no damage to Muzzle Wisps and Muzzle Flares.; Due to the high knockback and large size of the fireballs, they can hit the same enemy multiple times.

entries.englishGameplaySummary: The shotbow fires crossbow bolts in a shotgun burst. The shotbow is the result of a failed experiment using the Gungeon's teleportation network.
entries.englishSynergyHighlights: Second Accident If the player has Teleporter Prototype or Chest Teleporter, Shotbow fires 9 parallel arrows straight forward.
entries.englishUsageNotes: The Shotbow is in the SHOTGUN gun class and as such benefits from the Shotgun Affinity synergy.; This gun's description could be a reference to the episode Tuvix, from the TV show Star Trek Voyager, where two characters were merged into one as a result of a transporter (a sort of teleporter) accident, which is itself a reference to the film The Fly.

entries.englishGameplaySummary: Just some junk.
entries.englishEffectHighlights: Junk has no inherent effect, however it does interact with some other systems and items.; When playing as The Robot, each piece of Junk increases damage by a permanent additive 5%. The damage increase persists no matter what happens to the Junk.
entries.englishSynergyHighlights: Grouch Junk increases the damage of the Trashcannon by 25%. This effect does not stack with multiple Junk.
entries.englishUsageNotes: Junk has a chest destruction loot weight of 0.45.; If the player is in possession of Ser Junkan, the chest destruction loot weight of junk is multiplied by 1.5.

entries.englishGameplaySummary: After mostly confirming the functionality of bounce physics, work was done to improve the design. Smaller, lighter, and in an attractive shade of purple, the RUBE-ADYNE MK. II delivers on the promise of the original prototype.
entries.englishEffectHighlights: Fires purple lasers that can each bounce up to 2 times.
entries.englishSynergyHighlights: Rubenstein's Monster If the player also has RUBE-ADYNE Prototype, the two guns are combined to form Rubenstein's Monster.; This synergy isn't granted with Lich's Eye Bullets.
entries.englishUsageNotes: RUBE-ADYNE MK.II is the upgraded version of the RUBE-ADYNE Prototype.; The RUBE-ABYNE MK.II multiplies the loot weight of the RUBE-ADYNE Prototype by 2, doubling its likelihood of appearing. This is likely to aid players in obtaining the Rubenstein's Monster synergy.

entries.unlock: Purchase from Trorc for 8 HC.
entries.englishGameplaySummary: Standard box rocket. Capacity: 4. Often found atop military transports or mechanized walkers, this box rocket can be fired manually by the strongest Gungeoneers.
entries.englishEffectHighlights: Fires explosive 220 missiles.
entries.englishSynergyHighlights: Behold! If the player also has Eye of the Beholster, M1911, Void Marshal, Machine Pistol, and Trank Gun (the weapons the Beholster uses in game), when the Eye of the Beholster is held, the other five guns rotate around the player and shoot at nearby enemies, still using up ammo from each gun. Obtainable through the Beholster shrine, although it can be activated without.; Commammo Belt If the player also has Ammo Belt, Com4nd0 gains double magazine size and changes appearance, becoming light green and featuring an ammo belt around it.
entries.englishUsageNotes: This gun is one of the six wielded by the Beholster. When used by the Beholster, it fires homing rockets visually identical to those fired by the player.; As it is held by the Beholster, it can be placed on the Beholster Shrine.

entries.englishGameplaySummary: Projectiles shatter and freeze enemies.
entries.englishEffectHighlights: Fires bouncing ice cubes which leave trails of water, explode, and freeze enemies.; Like most explosions, these ice blasts can destroy enemy bullets.
entries.englishSynergyHighlights: Unsinkable If the player has Corsair, Corsair fires exploding Titanic ships.
entries.englishUsageNotes: The ice cubes bounce off Cursed Pots and will destroy them without exploding.; Prior to the Advanced Gungeons & Draguns Update, Glacier was a C gun.

entries.englishGameplaySummary: Carry an additional item and more ammunition.
entries.englishEffectHighlights: Grants an extra active item slot.; Increases maximum ammo by 20%.
entries.englishUsageNotes: Gunnerang If the player also has Boomerang, thrown guns deal 50% more damage.; M1 Multi-Tool If the player also has M1, M1's reload time is decreased by 20%, and its magazine size and maximum ammo are increased. Movement speed is also increased while M1 is held.

entries.englishGameplaySummary: Increases health substantially. Any purchases will shatter the ring. Before the Shopkeep opened his shop, he was an avaricious and miserly man. He remains careful about any expenditures, but through capitalism he has purged himself of negative emotion.
entries.englishEffectHighlights: Grants two heart containers. Disappears upon purchasing anything from any shop.
entries.englishUsageNotes: Porkulent If the player has Pig, purchases will not destroy the ring.; The item disappears if it is dropped.

entries.englishGameplaySummary: The Backpack grants you the use of another Active Item. Useful, but cumbersome.
entries.englishEffectHighlights: Allows the player to hold an extra active item.; The active items are switched by default by pressing Shift (keyboard) or up on the D-pad (controller).
entries.englishUsageNotes: M1 Multi-Tool If the player also has M1, its reload time is decreased by 20%, and its magazine size and maximum ammo are increased. Movement speed is also increased while M1 is held.; Mak Pak If the player has Makarov, Makarov's maximum ammo is increased by 30%. While holding Marakov, it occasionally fires a homing bullet at an enemy for free.

entries.englishGameplaySummary: Ammo capacity increased.
entries.englishEffectHighlights: Increases maximum ammo by 20%.
entries.englishSynergyHighlights: Buckle Up If the player has Gungine, continuously moving with Gungine held gives the player a yellow glow, significantly increasing Gungine's rate of fire. The glow disappears if the player stops moving. Additionally, Gungine has a chance to not consume ammo upon firing.; Comammo Belt If the player has Com4nd0, its magazine size is doubled.
entries.englishUsageNotes: The M1 Multi-Tool synergy is a possible reference to the MIDA Multi-Tool, a gun from the game Destiny.; An Ammo Belt can be seen among the pile of items that makes up the Resourceful Rats throne in the Resourceful Rat's Lair.

entries.englishGameplaySummary: Enables active reload.
entries.englishEffectHighlights: When reloading, pressing the fire or reload buttons when the indicator passes a certain mark on the reload bar will instantly reload the weapon. The mark on the reload bar is set to be at 2/3 of the length of the reload bar/reload time.; This effectively decreases the reload time.
entries.englishSynergyHighlights: Chest High is the Best High If the player also has Mourning Star, damage is increased by 25% and the Macho Brace effect is activated after flipping a table. This version of the Macho Brace effect is different to the actual item's, as it increases damage by 100% for a brief time rather than Macho Brace's 30%.; This effect is triggered at the same time as and stacks with that of Table Tech Rage, increasing the damage even further.
entries.englishUsageNotes: Prior to the Supply Drop Update, Cog of Battle was a D item.; All active reload effects (including those of the Cog of Battle) are disabled while the Gun Queue challenge modifier is active.

entries.englishGameplaySummary: C4 can be placed and then triggered remotely. More useful than a standard time bomb, but less convenient than a proximity mine, plastic explosives can be manually triggered.
entries.englishEffectHighlights: Places explosives on the ground which then can be detonated by using the item again, dealing 60 damage to nearby enemies. Unlike Bomb, does not open secret rooms.
entries.englishUsageNotes: The C4 in game is based on the real-life plastic explosive. C4 is often known for its moldable clay-like quality; however, this doesn't appear in-game.; After placing down the C4 charge, the items appearance in the players UI will change to a transmitter switch until the mine is detonated.

entries.englishGameplaySummary: A map of the current layout of one floor of the Gungeon. Becomes obsolete quickly.
entries.englishEffectHighlights: Fully reveals the layout of the current floor, including secret rooms.
entries.englishSynergyHighlights: Parchmental If the player has Origuni, it fires two planes per shot. This lasts for the rest of the run even though the Map only lasts for one floor.
entries.englishUsageNotes: Map is consumed upon pickup and does not appear in the player's inventory.; Has a small chance to drop on room clear.

entries.englishGameplaySummary: This honeycomb bonds with the user, releasing bees to protect their hive when damaged.
entries.englishEffectHighlights: Taking damage spawns 12-20 150 bees that seek out and attack enemies. Bees deal 3 damage on impact and apply a lingering 'swarm' effect that deals 1 damage right away, and deals 1 damage every second for 2 more seconds.; These bees are not affected by the player's other items, or any of their stats except for bullet scale.
entries.englishSynergyHighlights: Bee Plus If the player has Jar of Bees, taking damage causes Honeycomb to spawn hornets, which travel faster and deal more damage than regular bees. Jar of Bees will also spawn hornets instead of bees.; BEES If the player has Bee Hive, the size of the Bee Hive's bees is doubled, their damage is increased by 25%, and the Bee Hive's rate of fire is decreased by 34%.
entries.englishUsageNotes: Prior to the Supply Drop Update, Honeycomb was a B item.; This item is a reference to The Pain, a character in Metal Gear Solid 3: Snake Eater capable of controlling insects due to a parasite infection giving him the ability to secrete pheromones. It may be a further reference to special 'bullet bees', gestated by The Pain inside his own body and released through his mouth.

entries.englishGameplaySummary: Increases the chance of gaining a key upon clearing a room.
entries.englishEffectHighlights: Has a 20% chance to give a free Key upon clearing a room if no damage was taken in that room.
entries.englishSynergyHighlights: Firing With Flair If the player also has the Flare Gun, it turns green and is guaranteed to inflict enemies with greenfire, which has a massively increased duration.; Key Witness If the player also has Shelleton Key, firing any gun has a chance to fire an AKEY-47 shot.
entries.englishUsageNotes: In practice, this item can almost fully substitute for infinite unlock items due to the sheer amount of keys it generates (assuming the player has little to no Curse).; This item is a reference to the character Jill Valentine from Resident Evil and her ability to pick locks.

entries.englishGameplaySummary: A prototype ricochet blaster. This blaster was created to test the limits of projectile impact and ricochet physics.
entries.englishSynergyHighlights: Rubenstein's Monster If the player also has RUBE-ADYNE MK.II, the two guns are combined to form Rubenstein's Monster.; This synergy isn't granted by Lich's Eye Bullets.
entries.englishUsageNotes: The RUBE-ADYNE MK.II is an upgraded version of this gun.; The RUBE-ADYNE weapons may be named after David Rubel, one of the programmers of Enter the Gungeon.

entries.englishGameplaySummary: A merciless shotgun variant, packed with skulls, nails, and poison. After Hespera fell, her shotgun grew dark and corrupted by rage.
entries.englishEffectHighlights: Fires a spread of 6 disparate bullets with different effects.; 2 regular bullets with lower range than the other projectiles.
entries.englishSynergyHighlights: Careful Iteration If the player also has Casey, Casey becomes spiked and fires a spread of 6 nails each time it is swung.; Love/Hate If the player also has Shotgun Full of Love, the two guns are dual-wielded.
entries.englishUsageNotes: The Shotgun Full of Hate being in the SHOTGUN gun class makes it inherently less common than its counterpart, the Shotgun Full of Love which exists in the CHARM class.; This is due to the SHOTGUN class being a more common class, meaning more guns exist where possessing them will make the Shotgun Full of Hate less common.

entries.englishGameplaySummary: Chance to transmogrify. The Witch Pistol is a hexed variant of a standard magnum. A favorite of magic users seeking to become familiar with gunplay.
entries.englishEffectHighlights: Each bullet fired has a 10% chance to transmogrify enemies into harmless chickens.
entries.englishSynergyHighlights: Cast Iron If the player has Big Iron, each of Big Iron's bullets are replaced with one from Bundle of Wands, Witch Pistol, and Hexagun.; Chicken Arise If the player has Magic Bullets, bullet size and transmogrification chances are increased.
entries.englishUsageNotes: Bosses and Mimics are immune to transmogrification.; Bullets from the Witch Pistol leave R, N, and Φ shaped scorch marks on impacting a north wall.

entries.unlock: Defeat the High Dragun.
entries.englishGameplaySummary: Finely balanced, rapid fire. Can ignite foes. An Edwin original. The Dragunfire was forged deep in the heart of the Gungeon, crafted to emulate the wrath of the beast from which it takes its name.
entries.englishSynergyHighlights: Dragunice If the player has the Heart of Ice, the Dragunfire will shoot blue fire that has a chance to burn and/or freeze an enemy.; Kalibreath If the player has Yellow Chamber or Holey Grail, the Dragunfire and its projectiles turn gray. It gains increased rate of fire and knockback, but loses piercing projectiles.
entries.englishUsageNotes: The design of the Dragunfire is that of the Bolter from the dystopian science-fantasy universe of Warhammer 40,000.; The name of the gun comes from the Dragonfire round which the Bolter can fire. Dragonfire Bolts are hollow-shelled bolts that explode with a gout of superheated gas that can eliminate the value of cover.

entries.englishGameplaySummary: Just some junk. You feel bad about this junk, though.
entries.englishEffectHighlights: None.
entries.englishUsageNotes: Betrayer's Lies If the player has Betrayer's Shield, it gains infinite ammo, a much larger magazine size, increased accuracy, and extremely high fire rate.; Wicked Sister If the player also has Magnum, Magnum gets 200 max ammo and a knife on the front of the gun that will block bullets when reloading.

stats.value: 40 (Assuming an enemy is only hit by one stream of notes)

entries.unlock: Purchase from Ox and Cadence for 10 HC.
entries.englishGameplaySummary: A normal electric guitar. Drops an amplifier on reload for double the power! Unfortunately, musical talent and Gungeoneering skill do not often overlap. Any damage caused by this "gun" has little to do with the Gungeon.
entries.englishEffectHighlights: Simultaneously fires four streams of musical notes at 90° intervals.; Creates an amplifier on the ground when reloading. While firing, the active amplifier will fire bullets of its own in sync with the main gun, including aim angle.
entries.englishSynergyHighlights: Alternative Rock If the player has Drum Clip, Melted Rock, Gilded Bullets, Platinum Bullets, Heavy Bullets, or Ruby Bracelet, instead of firing four streams, Face Melter fires two parallel columns of notes. Reloading fires a guitar pick with a chance to stun. The pick can reveal secret rooms. This synergy also gives Face Melter a new, blue sprite.; When the guitar pick from this synergy hits an enemy it will cause them to bleed. This effect is purely visual.
entries.englishUsageNotes: Amplifiers do not have hitboxes, meaning that enemies can potentially stand on top of them and be damaged by all four note streams at once. This is particularly easy to achieve with enemies with large hitboxes, such as bosses.; Bug: In co-op, if playing as The Cultist, reloading will drop the amplifier at the other player's location.

entries.englishGameplaySummary: Fires T-Shirts. Gungeon Merch was a shortlived startup enterprise founded by a few misguided Gundead. After shooting their fourth consecutive customer, they turned their sights away from business.
entries.englishSynergyHighlights: The Return If the player has Pig, it turns green to resemble Cannon, and fires flaming bats.; Well Dressed If the player also has Poxcannon, both guns fire explosive wardrobes at the end of their clips.
entries.englishUsageNotes: This weapon and its high knockback are a reference to an episode of The Simpsons, in which Maude Flanders is shot by a T-Shirt Cannon and falls to her death.Confirmed in this tweet from Dave CrooksArchived Tweet; The T-Shirt Cannon was first teased in a twitter post from Dodge Roll on 01/02/2015.

entries.unlock: Purchase from Professor Goopton for 10 HC.
entries.englishGameplaySummary: Fires a splitting burst of mucus. Brought to the Gungeon by the warlike Blobulonians, its suppressive fire helped King Visc Osbro III win back the province of Goopscone from the rebel forces led by his brother, Gel Osbro.
entries.englishSynergyHighlights: Absent Minded If the player also has Bouncy Bullets, The Membrane's shots bounce 4 times and become homing.; This synergy does not work with Lich's Eye Bullets.
entries.englishUsageNotes: The Absent Minded synergy is a reference to the movie The Absent-Minded Professor, which features an extremely bouncy material known as Flubber. It is also possibly a reference to the television show Invader Zim, which has a character named Professor Membrane.; Due to its overpenetration, high range, and high projectile count, the Membrane works extremely well with Flak Bullets and Scattershot.

entries.englishGameplaySummary: Gungeoneers tend to have little regard for the decorative pottery found throughout the Gungeon. To help quickly replace the broken decorations, custodians commissioned this pot producing pistol. This earliest version fired the pots with too much velocity, and was therefore discarded.
entries.englishSynergyHighlights: Bullet Kiln If the player has Elder Blank, using it while The Kiln is held gives one Glass Guon Stone, up to a maximum of four.; The limit can be worked around by dropping excess glass guon stones on the floor before using the Elder Blank, and then collecting them again.
entries.englishUsageNotes: This gun is a reference to The Legend of Zelda series, where thrown pots can be used to deal damage.; The crystals that the pots break into are also a reference to rupees in The Legend of Zelda.

entries.englishGameplaySummary: The Shock Rifle uses a modified battery and coil to propel electricity.
entries.englishUsageNotes: Assault and Battery If the player also has the M16, the guns are dual wielded.; Battery Powered If the player has R2G2 or Shock Rounds, Shock Rifle gains a smartphone battery-esque look and automatically regenerates 1 ammo every 4 seconds.

entries.unlock: Reach the Oubliette.
entries.englishGameplaySummary: An improvised gun that fires trash, sending poison in all directions. A threat to anyone in the room, including the wielder. Becomes more powerful the more Junk a Gungeoneer is carrying.
entries.englishUsageNotes: Garbage Collecting If the player has Unity, hitting an enemy with Trashcannon causes it and its bullets to briefly freeze. Bullets slow down to a very low percentage of its original travel speed, having a very slight amount of motion.; Grouch Junk increases the damage of the Trashcannon by 25%. This effect does not stack with multiple Junk (despite the Ammonomicon entry claiming otherwise).

entries.unlock: Purchase from Ox and Cadence for 12 HC.
entries.englishGameplaySummary: Creates a small black hole, damaging any enemies and destroying any projectiles caught in its gravitational pull. First pioneered by the Hegemony of Man during the Ten Days' War, the Portable Star Crusher was deemed too dangerous for broad deployment.
entries.englishEffectHighlights: Spawns a black hole that sucks enemies, bullets, and items into it.; Enemies in the black hole take damage.
entries.englishSynergyHighlights: Hellhole If the player has Yellow Chamber, Sixth Chamber, or Holey Grail, Singularity spawns a hell portal that sucks enemies in and deals high damage.; Outer Limits If the player has Mr. Accretion Jr., firing three planets into Singularity's black hole causes it to explode and deal 100 damage to all enemies in the room.
entries.englishUsageNotes: Bosses are not pulled into the black hole.; Consumes any HC spawned in its proximity.

entries.englishGameplaySummary: Pierce Weapon. Gifted to any who reach Level 5 in the Order of the True Gun.
entries.englishEffectHighlights: Fires blue laser blasts capable of each piercing 1 enemy.; These lasers electrify any water that they pass over.
entries.englishSynergyHighlights: Lotus Bloom If the player has Rolling Eye or Shock Rounds, the Laser Lotus gains the appearance of a flower. Its magazine size increases to 32, its maximum ammo increases to 300, its fire rate greatly increases, and it deals half damage.; Particle Flow If player also has Laser Sight, Laser Lotus gains a guided form of homing. A targeting reticle appears on the enemy nearest to the mouse and shots will home towards that enemy.
entries.englishUsageNotes: This gun is a reference to Community. The character Pierce is a "Level 5 Laser Lotus" in his Buddhist renewal community.

entries.englishGameplaySummary: The Big Iron is a strange revolver, created by attaching additional barrels to a magnum. The barrels are not actually connected to the chamber, but they fire nonetheless. Decades ago, Bullet Kin learned of the existence of human gunsmiths. In the intervening years, they have attempted to replicate the art of guncraft with mixed results.
entries.englishSynergyHighlights: Cast Iron If the player has Bundle of Wands, Witch Pistol, or Hexagun, each of Big Iron's bullets are replaced with one from Bundle of Wands, Witch Pistol, and Hexagun. These bullets have infinite range.; Iron Slug If the player also has Stout Bullets, Scope, Heavy Boots, Heavy Bullets, or Fat Bullets, Big Iron fires one large, explosive iron bullet that does increased damage.
entries.englishUsageNotes: Despite Big Iron using bullets in its ammo bar, shell cartridges come out of its compartment when reloading.; Despite firing a spread of multiple bullets like a shotgun, the Big Iron is not in the SHOTGUN gun class, instead residing in the PISTOL class.

entries.unlock: Kill 100 enemies by knocking them into pits.
entries.englishGameplaySummary: Prevents all fall damage. No matter how skilled, every adventurer makes mistakes. Falling into a pit is perhaps the most irritating example. This amulet represents a bargain with the Pit Lord, avatar of the depths.
entries.englishEffectHighlights: Falling into pits no longer deals damage.
entries.englishSynergyHighlights: Bluer Guon Stone If the player also has Blue Guon Stone, the rotation speed of the Guon Stone is increased but it orbits farther from the player. If the Guon Stone blocks a bullet, it deals 15 damage to all nearby enemies.; Clearer Guon Stone If the player also has Clear Guon Stone, it grants immunity to poison, fire, and electricity. Shooting enemies while standing on fire/oil or ice/water will burn or freeze them, respectively.

entries.unlock: Purchase from Professor Goopton for 25 HC.
entries.englishGameplaySummary: Reduces enemy health. Disease spreads quickly among the Gundead, as they sleep tightly packed together in magazines.
entries.englishEffectHighlights: Reduces the health of all enemies and bosses by 25%.
entries.englishSynergyHighlights: Pandemic Pistol If the player also has Plague Pistol, the pistol turns into the Pandemic Pistol, turning red with an increased ammo capacity, and its bullets become connected by a damaging electric link.
entries.englishUsageNotes: This item reduces the health of both normal enemies and bosses.; Gundromeda Strain's effect is equivalent to increasing damage by 33%.

entries.unlock: Reward from [[Frifle_and_the_Grey_Mauser#List_of_Quests
entries.englishGameplaySummary: The helmet of the Aimless Knight, Cormorant. Cormorant, upon reaching the great Forge, removed his helmet and set it upon the floor. When he realized his mistake and returned for the helm, it was gone, with only a note left in its place.
entries.englishEffectHighlights: Grants two pieces of armor upon pickup.; Grants a piece of armor upon entering a new floor.
entries.englishUsageNotes: Cormorant Acquiring all four Gunknight items transforms the player into Cormorant.; Having this item increases the chance to find other Gunknight items.

entries.englishGameplaySummary: The greaves of the Aimless Knight, Cormorant. The Aimless Knight approached the Gungeon as a challenge borne more out of curiosity than regret. Despite his skill, this rendered his failure inevitable.
entries.englishEffectHighlights: Grants a piece of Armor upon pickup.; Grants a piece of Armor upon entering a new floor.
entries.englishSynergyHighlights: Cormorant Acquiring all four Gunknight items transforms the player into Cormorant.; Knight Time If the player has Ballistic Boots, taking damage briefly stops time in a manner similar to the Aged Bell.
entries.englishUsageNotes: Having this item increases the chance to find other Gunknight items.; One of the projectiles that can be fired by Pedestal Mimics takes the form of a spinning Gunknight Greave.

entries.englishGameplaySummary: The gauntlets of the Aimless Knight, Cormorant. Though the Aimless Knight never needed to reload, even his prodigious skill was not enough to best the beast that lies at the base of the Gungeon.
entries.englishEffectHighlights: Grants a piece of armor upon pickup.; Grants a piece of armor upon entering a new floor.
entries.englishUsageNotes: Clawntlet If the player has Cat Claw, Gunknight Gauntlet will grant 3 armor instead of 1, and taking damage causes bullets from the currently held gun to be sprayed in all directions.; Cormorant Acquiring all four Gunknight items transforms the player into Cormorant.

entries.englishGameplaySummary: The breastplate of the Aimless Knight, Cormorant. Able to dispatch the Gundead with ease, the Knight grew overconfident in his abilities. Deep in the Hollow, his concentration had weakened, and he sustained a grievous wound.
entries.englishEffectHighlights: Grants a piece of armor upon pickup.; Grants a piece of armor upon entering a new floor.
entries.englishUsageNotes: Cormorant Acquiring all four Gunknight items transforms the player into Cormorant.; Having this item increases the chance to find other Gunknight items.

entries.englishGameplaySummary: Extra chance for hearts on room clear.
entries.englishEffectHighlights: If the player is not at full health, adds a 20% chance to gain a Half Heart upon completing a room without taking damage.
entries.englishSynergyHighlights: Synthetic Shield If the player has Armor Synthesizer or Ammo Synthesizer, grants a familiar that orbits the player and blocks shots. If all three synthesizers are picked up, grants three familiars.; The familiar follows the player with almost no delay and represents the synthesizer the player does not currently have (i.e. if the player has Armor Synthesizer and Heart Synthesizer, it will appear to be an Ammo box).
entries.englishUsageNotes: Heart Synthesizer has no benefit for The Robot, as it cannot use hearts. It still does, however, contribute to the synergy mentioned above.; In the Chinese Mobile Version of Enter the Gungeon, the sprite of Heart Synthesizer has been changed to resemble the logo of Chinese game developer XD, the company responsible for the mobile port.

entries.englishGameplaySummary: Increases the speed at which guns can be reloaded.
entries.englishEffectHighlights: Decreases reload time by 50%.
entries.englishSynergyHighlights: Hail To The King If the player also has the Yari Launcher, its magazine size is increased by 50% and its damage is increased by 20%.; Not Quite As Mini If the player also has Vulcan Cannon, its maximum ammo is increased by 100 and its rate of fire is significantly increased.

entries.unlock: Steal 10 items from shops.
entries.englishGameplaySummary: Opens any lock. Never consumed. The ultimate tool in the Gungeon Acquisitions' Department's arsenal.
entries.englishEffectHighlights: Allows any chest or locked door to be opened without using a Key.; Increases by 1 while held.
entries.englishSynergyHighlights: Akey Breaky If the player has the AKEY-47, the AKEY-47 receives a damage boost, infinite ammo, changes in appearance to resemble a Shelleton, and can no longer open locks by shooting at them.; Key Witness If the player has Master of Unlocking, guns will periodically fire an AKEY-47 shot.
entries.englishUsageNotes: The Shelleton Key can only substitute regular Keys, and cannot open locks that require special keys.; This includes Cell locks (which require Cell Keys), the hatch to the Resourceful Rat's Lair (which requires the Gnawed Key), and Rat Chests/Locks (which require Rat Keys).

entries.unlock: Purchase from Ox and Cadence for 7 HC.
entries.englishGameplaySummary: Slows enemy projectiles. This intoxicating substance grants the user vision that slows down enemy bullet speeds. Gun Cultists use it in their dark rituals, hoping to gain the favor of the Order of the True Gun.
entries.englishEffectHighlights: Decreases enemy bullet speed by 15%.
entries.englishSynergyHighlights: Fear the Old Blood If the player has Trick Gun, its reload time is decreased by 33% and its fire rate is increased by 20%.; Hidden Tech Time If the player has Table Tech Sight, flipping a table briefly stops time. This only stops enemies currently on the screen, new ones spawning (e.g. by a Blobulon splitting) will not be stopped.
entries.englishUsageNotes: This item can be counterproductive; since bullets are slower than usual, the time the bullets are in midair is increased, meaning the player can run into bullets that would have already disappeared without Bloody Eye. This is noticeable when fighting Old King while he does his spinning attack; the bullets move slower at the same spread, making it harder to navigate through the gaps. The same applies to Liquid Valkyrie.; This item's name, appearance and effects are a reference to particularly potent form of a drug named Red Eye in Cowboy Bebop which, when taken, could heighten the user's reaction time to the point where they perceive time in slow motion.

entries.englishGameplaySummary: Temporarily doubles gun output. The favored brew of Two-Shot Erias. During her time in the Gungeon, she claimed that drinking before a gunfight would double her effectiveness in combat, but for obvious reasons was not taken too seriously. Perhaps there was some truth in her tales.
entries.englishEffectHighlights: While active, doubles all bullets fired by the player, but reduces accuracy.
entries.englishSynergyHighlights: Dwarven Stout If the player also has Stout Bullets, Double Vision will triple shots fired instead of double.; Five O'Clock Somewhere If the player also has Crestfaller, reloading will fire a glass bottle that has a chance to stun enemies hit. Reloading Crestfaller while standing on a liquid replenishes the ammo missing in the current magazine, similar to Gungine.
entries.englishUsageNotes: If you have multiple active items, switching off Double Vision while active will end the effect prematurely.; The two bullets fired can be desynced by using the item while the current gun is in-between shots. This is easiest to achieve with slow firing weapons like Sniper Rifle or A.W.P..

entries.unlock: Purchase from Ox and Cadence for 20 HC.
entries.englishGameplaySummary: Shoots Black Holes. A technological marvel, produced by Interstice Technology. Once fully charged, users report seeing the momentary birth, and death, of a star.
entries.englishUsageNotes: Cosmic Horror If the player has Abyssal Tentacle, black holes fire three tentacles while active.; The black hole will also attract friendly projectiles; however, beam type weapons will not be affected.

entries.englishGameplaySummary: Active items begin their cooldown period sooner and cool down faster. Most items in the Gungeon overheat for a brief time when used. This ice cube is perfect for cooling them off!
entries.englishEffectHighlights: Allows active items to recharge while they are active.; Increases Coolness by 3.
entries.englishUsageNotes: Ice To Meet You If the player also has Heart of Ice, firing with any gun will periodically fire a Heart of Ice projectile that splits and bounces. Like regular Heart of Ice projectiles, these projectiles can freeze water.; The probability of firing the ice projectile appears to be inversely proportional to the gun's fire rate. Curiously, it will always fire if the player swings Blasphemy with full hearts.

entries.englishGameplaySummary: Grants all bullets piercing.
entries.englishEffectHighlights: Bullets pierce through enemies and pass through obstacles (including tables).; Each bullet can pierce through one enemy.
entries.englishSynergyHighlights: Electron Pack Kills with the electric beam of the Proton Backpack spawn Gunslinger's Ashes ghosts that attack enemies. This does not activate if enemies are killed by the Proton Backpack's other beam.; Ghost With the Most Gunslinger's Ashes reload speed increased by 20%. Gunslinger's Ashes ghosts will be larger, and the shots they fire will freeze enemies.

entries.englishGameplaySummary: Temporarily increases combat ability. Another of Goopton's inventions. The Potion of Gun Friendship will temporarily strengthen one's bond with their weapon, increasing combat prowess significantly.
entries.englishEffectHighlights: While active, increases damage by 30%, doubles fire rate, decreases reload times by 70%, and multiplies knockback by 10.
entries.englishSynergyHighlights: Five O'Clock Somewhere If the player also has Crestfaller, reloading will fire a glass bottle that has a chance to stun enemies hit. Reloading Crestfaller while standing on a liquid replenishes the ammo missing in the current magazine, similar to Gungine.; Friend to Gun and Bullet If the player has +1 Bullets, while Potion of Gun Friendship is active, a copy of the player's current gun appears and automatically fires at nearby enemies.
entries.englishUsageNotes: Switching to another active item while the Potion of Gun Friendship is active will prematurely end the effect.; Prior to the A Farewell to Arms update, if the player were to drop Potion of Gun Friendship while the Friend to Gun and Bullet was active, the copy gun became permanent. This could be done repeatedly to amass a large amount of orbiting guns.

entries.unlock: Purchase from Ox and Cadence for 10 HC.
entries.englishGameplaySummary: Rectangles enemies. The Tangler shotgun fires particles from a dimension where matter exists only in rectilinear form.
entries.englishEffectHighlights: Fires shotgun blasts of rectangular bullets that bounce off walls.; Enemies hit will gain a rectangular appearance.
entries.englishSynergyHighlights: Iron Stance If the player also has Heavy Boots, the gun's damage and knockback are increased by 20%, its shot speed is increased by 50%, and its reload time is decreased by 20%.; Primitive Shapes If the player also has Quad Laser, Tangler's shots become much larger and deal 50% more damage, but travel very slowly. Quad Laser's shots become rectangles and travel faster.
entries.englishUsageNotes: Despite being a shotgun, the Tangler is in the SILLY gun class.; This means that unlike most other shotguns, the Tangler is not affected by the Shotgun Affinity synergy.

entries.englishGameplaySummary: Alternates between oily bursts and fiery blasts. When the great Bullet fell from the sky, not even the earth beneath the Gungeon was spared. The ants that made their home beneath the floors became terrifying in size, and capable of projectile spit. Nearly extinct, the last of the species occasionally hide in chests.
entries.englishSynergyHighlights: Buggin' Out If the player has Bug Boots, while Gungeon Ant is held, Bug Boots spread oil instead of poison, and the player is immune to fire.; Great Queen Ant If the player also has Crown of Guns, Heck Blaster, Gilded Bullets, Coin Crown, or Ring of Fire Resistance, Gungeon Ant loses the alternating attack and instead fires explosive ants that destroy enemy bullets when exploding and deal 22 impact damage, 35 explosion damage.
entries.englishUsageNotes: This gun is a reference to the real-life Bullet Ant. Known for its extremely painful sting, victims have likened it to "being shot".; The Great Queen Ant synergy is a reference to Earthworm Jim, specifically The Evil Queen Pulsating, Bloated, Festering, Sweaty, Pus-filled, Malformed, Slug-for-a-Butt.

entries.englishGameplaySummary: The engine of a crashed starship that has been converted into an energy weapon.
entries.englishEffectHighlights: Rapidly fires tiny invisible pellets with a very short range, extending only slightly past the gun's visible muzzle flash.; Sets enemies on fire.
entries.englishSynergyHighlights: Ancient Aliens Zorgun gets three special shots at the end of each magazine instead of one. Alien Engine will also fire Zorgun bullets.; Contrail or Mutation The Alien Engine changes forms, and its muzzle flash becomes green, igniting enemies with greenfire. Alien Engine also grants flight while held.
entries.englishUsageNotes: Despite its downsides, this gun has among the highest DPS in the Enter the Gungeon, making it useful for hit-and-run tactics in a Boss battle, or in combination with a longer range weapon.; In September of 2016, Dodge Roll programmer Brent Sodman listed the Alien Engine as his favourite gun in Enter the Gungeon, and Shotgrub as his least favourite. He did however disclaim that his opinions on Enter the Gungeons weapons change often, so these preferences may have changed over time and with the addition of more weapons."fav: alien engine least fav: shotgrub though honestly it changes from day to day" - Brent Sodman in a Twitter Post

entries.englishGameplaySummary: Freezes enemies. This unique weapon was brought to the Gungeon by a disgraced warrior who discarded it when he gave up. The projectiles it fires are extremely low-energy, delivering a cold and highly depressing death.
entries.englishUsageNotes: Crestfallen Soul If the player also has Gun Soul, dodge roll speed is increased by 20% and Crestfaller deals 50% more damage if the player dies and has not yet retrieved their soul.; Five O'Clock Somewhere If the player also has Shotga Cola, Shotgun Coffee, Double Vision, Potion of Gun Friendship, Potion of Lead Skin, or Old Knight's Flask, reloading will fire a glass bottle that has a chance to stun enemies hit. Reloading while standing on a liquid replenishes the ammo missing in the current magazine, similar to Gungine.

entries.englishGameplaySummary: Deals extra damage to ghosts and spectres. Originally brought to the Gungeon by a team of investigators who suspected that something strange was going on.
entries.englishEffectHighlights: Fires two slow-moving simultaneous beams;; A thick 420 red laser that deals very low damage.
entries.englishSynergyHighlights: Electron Pack or Ghost Bullets Kills with electric beam of the Proton Backpack spawn Gunslinger's Ashes ghosts that attack enemies. This does not activate if enemies are killed by the Proton Backpack's other beam.
entries.englishUsageNotes: This gun is a reference to the iconic Proton Pack ghost fighting weapon from the Ghostbusters franchise.; The Ammonomicon quote "Crossing Streams" is in reference to the repeated warning in Ghostbusters (1984) "Don't cross the streams", as crossing the streams from multiple Proton Backpacks can result in a cataclysm called 'total proton reversal', destroying all life.

entries.unlock: Purchase from Ox and Cadence for 8 HC.
entries.englishGameplaySummary: This petite weapon packs a punch, and the recoil can knock the user backwards.
entries.englishSynergyHighlights: To Serve Android If the player has Badge, the police officer turns into a robot and becomes invincible.
entries.englishUsageNotes: The explosions created on impact are not blanks, despite being able to delete bullets. They will not open secret rooms or trigger Ammolets.; If a Gunjurer catches and returns a shot from this gun, the blank effect will still damage the player.

entries.englishGameplaySummary: The Winchester Rifle was used to famous effect by Frifle during his first adventures with the Grey Mauser. It might be a bit dated, but still packs quite the punch.
entries.englishSynergyHighlights: Diazepam Increases damage by 50% while the Winchester Rifle is held.; Guns and Deviltry If the player also has Grey Mauser, damage is increased by 25% and it fires a knife when reloaded.
entries.englishUsageNotes: The Winchester Rifle appears to be based off the real world Winchester Model 1866, the first 'Winchester Rifle' created by the Winchester Repeating Arms Company. This gun was known for its distinctive yellow receiver, made of a bronze-brass alloy which earned it the nickname Yellow Boy.; Although classified as semi-automatic, the Winchester Rifle is functionally automatic, as its rate of fire does not benefit from tapping the fire button instead of holding it down.

entries.unlock: Reward from [[Frifle_and_the_Grey_Mauser#List_of_Quests
entries.englishGameplaySummary: Renders the wielder invisible when reloading an empty clip. Extra damage while stealthed.
entries.englishEffectHighlights: s the player upon reloading an empty clip. While stealthed, enemies do not see the player, and they may steal from merchants.; Deals 400% damage if fired while the bearer is stealthed.
entries.englishSynergyHighlights: Guns and Deviltry If the player also has Winchester Rifle, the damage of both guns is increased by 25% and when reloading each will fire a knife the direction of the reticle that deals 8 damage, can stun enemies, and causes an aesthetic bleed effect.
entries.englishUsageNotes: Followers like Wingman will not shoot while the invisibility effect is active.; This gun's name is a portmanteau of an early semi-automatic pistol design, the Mauser C96, and the thief from Fritz Leiber's fantasy books known as the Gray Mouser.

entries.englishGameplaySummary: This revolver was brought to the Gungeon by a fearless adventurer whose true quest is still unknown.
entries.englishUsageNotes: Ser Manuel's Revolver is wielded by Ser Manuel in his bossfight in the Halls of Knowledge.; Due to the way enemy AI is coded in Enter the Gungeon, companions and enemies which use weapons disconnected from their sprite hold real guns which are theoretically usable in the players inventory, albeit unobtainable. This may explain why Ser Manuel's Revolver is absent from all loot pools, despite being fully functional.

entries.englishGameplaySummary: Pass judgement on your enemies.
entries.englishEffectHighlights: A green bullet that deals 4 damage, poison enemies, and leaves a small puddle of poison goop.; A flare that deals 4 damage and sets enemies on fire.
entries.englishSynergyHighlights: To Serve Android The police officer turns into a robot and becomes invincible.
entries.englishUsageNotes: This gun is a reference to Judge Dredd's gun, the Lawgiver.; The pickup quote "Hot Shot" is one of the Lawgiver's settings.

entries.englishGameplaySummary: According to legend, two men were each fitted with one of these hands. One the left, and the other the right. The next time they met, only one walked away.
entries.englishEffectHighlights: With keyboard and mouse controls, the rocket fist will home in towards the players cursor.; With gamepad controls, the rocket will update its direction to match the currently held aim direction on the controller.
entries.englishSynergyHighlights: that &^#$# 'pizza' If the player also has RPG, RPG's rockets are replaced with a rocket fist that can be guided, and Machine Fist's bullets bounce twice and gain slight homing.
entries.englishUsageNotes: Like other single shot weapons, firing a rocket fist causes the player to reload the gun immediately after, transforming it.; In order to reload, the gun must have remaining ammo greater than the size of the magazine. Therefore, once the last of the gun's ammo is loaded in machine gun mode, none of that ammo can be used for rocket punches, even if there is more than 10 ammo remaining.

entries.unlock: Kill The Pilot's past.
entries.englishGameplaySummary: Reduces prices at shops. The Pilot is able to talk his way into almost anything, usually gunfights.
entries.englishEffectHighlights: Grants a 15% discount at shops.; Grants a 20% discount at shops.
entries.englishUsageNotes: Cannot be sold, even as gungeoneers other than The Pilot.; Does not provide a discount for the Gnawed Key or the Prime Primer.

entries.unlock: Retrieve Gunnut Butter and Pretzel Stick for Daisuke
entries.englishGameplaySummary: Rolling through bullets rebounds them toward their owners. Despite his many failings, Ser Manuel is one of the finest dodge rollers ever to enter the Gungeon's halls. This eye is almost purely composed of Dodgicite, a rare material that reacts strangely near bullets.
entries.englishEffectHighlights: Rolling through bullets reflects them at enemies.; Reflected bullets deal 10 damage.
entries.englishUsageNotes: Fear The Old Blood If the player has Trick Gun, its reload time is decreased by 33% and its fire rate is increased by 20%.; Iroquois If the player has MAC10, its reload time is decreased by 33%, its magazine size is increased by 50%, and its knockback is increased by 50%.

entries.englishGameplaySummary: Prevents damage from fire. A ring originally worn by the legendary gunsmith himself. Later in life, Edwin no longer needed it, but the ring proved indispensable during his early years in the Forge. It eventually passed to his eldest daughter.
entries.englishEffectHighlights: Grants immunity to fire.
entries.englishSynergyHighlights: Backdraft Touching enemies sets them on fire.; Great Queen Ant If the player has Gungeon Ant, it fires explosive rocket ants.

entries.unlock: Purchase from Professor Goopton for 7 HC.
entries.englishGameplaySummary: Creates poison on dodge roll, also grants immunity to poison.
entries.englishEffectHighlights: Dodge rolling leaves a trail of poison goop.; Grants immunity to poison.
entries.englishSynergyHighlights: Buggin' Out If the player has Gungeon Ant, while it is held, Bug Boots spread oil instead of poison, and the player becomes immune to fire.
entries.englishUsageNotes: Use of this item allows the player to spread poison over the entrance to Bullet Hell, despite there visually being no ground to spread it on.

entries.unlock: Purchase from Professor Goopton for 12 HC.
entries.englishGameplaySummary: Reloading this skull produces a gout of flame that can ignite oil. This is a spectacularly preserved specimen of a gun that went extinct during the Ballistac era.
entries.englishSynergyHighlights: Cryptic Cryptids If the player also has Phoenix, both guns gain piercing and deal more damage, and Phoenix shoots rows of fire on the ground. The player is also immune to fire when either gun is equipped.; Dawn of the Gundead Doubles the chance for Zombie Bullets to refund missed Fossilized Gun shots.
entries.englishUsageNotes: The plume of flame belched by the Fossilized Gun when reloading solely has the effect of igniting flammable goop. It does not harm enemies, trigger explosive barrels, or anger Bello. Additionally, the Fossilized Gun can be reloaded and made to spit fire regardless of its current ammo count- it can even be used this way when empty.; Is is possible to pre-clear rooms by firing blindly into them from the doorway without fully entering, and then igniting the placed oil. This strategy is however inconsistent due to the high variance in room layouts in Enter the Gungeon.

entries.unlock: Complete the tutorial.
entries.englishGameplaySummary: Traditionally given to Gungeoneers when they first arrive at the Gungeon. It is incredibly weak.
entries.englishSynergyHighlights: Greener Guon Stone If the player also has Green Guon Stone, the size of the guon stone is increased, it rotates at a fixed distance from the player while they are moving instead of on a slight delay, and the chance for the stone to heal is raised to 70% if the player is one hit from dying. 20 coin also appears whenever Green Guon Stone heals the player.; Pea Cannon If the player also has Old Knight's Helm, Old Knight's Shield, Crutch, Mahoguny, Starpew, Cactus, Wood Beam, or Broccoli, Pea Shooter's damage is increased and shots cause small explosions.
entries.englishUsageNotes: A peashooter is a type of toy blowgun. The term "pea shooter" is also used derogatorily to describe a weak, small-caliber firearm.; The Pea Shooter is given to you by Ser Manuel in the tutorial.

entries.unlock: Feed 10 guns to [[Munchers#Evil Munchers
entries.englishGameplaySummary: Releases a vengeful, and armed, spirit. The precise methods needed to preserve a body with gun intact have been lost to time, but the High Priest, First of the Gun, still remembers...
entries.englishEffectHighlights: Fires spectral gunslingers that slowly walk forwards and label=home towards nearby enemies.; Every 2 seconds, these gunslingers will fire a bullet at the nearest enemy which deals 5 damage. These spirits typically dissipate after shooting three times.
entries.englishSynergyHighlights: Dawn of the Gundead If the player has Zombie Bullets, the chance for Gunslinger's Ashes ammo to be refunded upon missing is doubled.; Ghost With The Most If the player has Ghost Bullets or Ring of Ethereal Form, the ghosts become larger and can freeze enemies. Reload time is decreased by 20%.
entries.englishUsageNotes: Due to the high damage dealt by the spirits themselves making contact with enemies, it is most effective to fire this gun at close range.; Despite this gun's name and the spirits' visible resemblance, it is unknown whether the spirit contained within this gun is The Gunslinger's or not.

entries.englishGameplaySummary: Rapid fire with a big finale. This tricky prism requires guile to wield, but with the right lens, fires shreds of solidified light that slice enemies in their path. Falsely rumored to have natural healing abilities.
entries.englishSynergyHighlights: Bullet Kiln If the player has Elder Blank, using it while Luxin Cannon is held gives one Glass Guon Stone, up to a maximum of four.; Uses of Elder Blank triggered by Full Metal Jacket do not grant a Glass Guon Stone.
entries.englishUsageNotes: This gun is a reference to The Lightbringer book series by Brent Weeks. The main character's last name is Guile, he is the Prism, Luxin is the name of the solidified light that is used by the magic users of that world, and Lord Omnichrome is a major antagonist of the series.; "Falsely rumored to have natural healing abilities" is a reference to crystal healing, a pseudoscientific alternative medicine technique that employs stones and crystals.

entries.englishGameplaySummary: Charms enemies temporarily. The preferred bow of the Pride of Venus before her fall. While its arrows deal little physical damage, they are coated in a potent hallucinogen. Even a slight scratch can confuse friend and foe.
entries.englishEffectHighlights: The lower level, requiring the gun to be charged for only 0.2 seconds, fires a mundane arrow.; The higher level, requiring the gun to be charged for 0.7 seconds, fires a faster moving and more damaging arrow with a heart trail. This arrow has a 75% chance to inflict the charm status effect on enemies it hits. This charm effect lasts for 6 seconds.
entries.englishSynergyHighlights: Cerebral Bros If the player also has Bullet Bore, Bullet Bore turns red and fires two drills at a time.; Pinker Guon Stone If the player has Pink Guon Stone, it grows in size, rotates at a fixed distance from the player while they are moving instead of on a slight delay, and enemies that damage the player become temporarily charmed.
entries.englishUsageNotes: Shots fired from the Orange Guon Stone and Space Friend while the Unbelievably Charming synergy is active are guaranteed to charm enemies.; The entry for the Charmed Bow in the Physical Ammonomicon has a small doodle of a winged Bullet Kin in the role of Cupid, carrying the weapon.

entries.unlock: Purchase from Ox and Cadence for 12 HC.
entries.englishGameplaySummary: Shoots at enemies for a short time. This portable turret is actually a doll commonly given to young Gundead. It shoots at anything nearby, and is thought to encourage good habits.
entries.englishEffectHighlights: Places a stationary turret that shoots at enemies and blocks enemy projectiles.; The turret will be destroyed if it takes enough damage.
entries.englishSynergyHighlights: Captain Plant It If the player has Unity, Portable Turret gains Unity's damage bonus.; Turret Link If the player has Chance Bullets, Portable Turret will sometimes fire shots from guns in the player's inventory.
entries.englishUsageNotes: If the player has the M1911 with Pulp Gungeon, Portable Turret's shots will make the M1911 circling around the player fire.; The effect stacks; if there are multiple Portable Turrets firing at once, the circling M1911 will shoot for each one firing.

entries.englishGameplaySummary: A shotgun modified for easy concealment. The shorter barrel widens the spread, but up-close, it's just as deadly as its full-barreled cousin.
entries.englishUsageNotes: The Sawed-Off is in the SHOTGUN gun class, and as such benefits from the Shotgun Affinity synergy.; This also means that, according to the rules of how loot is distributed, holding the Sawed-Off makes finding other SHOTGUN type weapons more rare. It may be in the player's best interests to drop the Sawed-Off to increase their chances of finding a different, better shotgun.

entries.englishGameplaySummary: Can result in serious health problems and even death. They do have a certain appeal though.
entries.englishEffectHighlights: Deals half a heart of damage to the player. This functions exactly the same as all other damage sources in the game, and will break Armor first.; Increases Coolness by 1 per use, decreasing the cooldown of active items and increasing the chance of items dropping upon clearing a room. The coolness bonus will remain for the entire run, even if the Cigarettes are removed from the player's inventory.
entries.englishSynergyHighlights: Iroquois If the player has MAC10, the MAC10's reload time is decreased by 33%, magazine size is increased by 50%, and knockback is increased by 50%.; What A Thrill If the player has Patriot, Patriot has a chance to transmogrify enemies into snakes.
entries.englishUsageNotes: Dying to Cigarettes lists "Smoking" as the cause of death on the Ammonomicon death page.; Using Cigarettes during a boss fight will count as taking damage, and will prevent the player from obtaining a Master Round.

entries.englishGameplaySummary: These irradiated slugs have a chance to poison any target they touch. A favorite of the Resourceful Rat.
entries.englishEffectHighlights: Adds a chance to fire green bullets that poison enemies for 2.5 seconds.; The chance for any given projectile to be poisoned is 50% multiplied by the effect chance scalar of the players current gun. This means that faster firing guns trigger the effect less often, while slower firing guns are more likely to be effected.
entries.englishSynergyHighlights: Added Effect - Poison If the player has Alpha Bullet, the first shot of each magazine is guaranteed to be a poison bullet.; Beta Ray Gamma Ray's beam grows larger and deals more damage, but no longer travels instantaneously.
entries.englishUsageNotes: If an explosive projectile (such as from the RPG or Explosive Rounds) is poisoned by Irradiated Lead, it will leave a small pool of poison goop upon exploding.; Emerald Weapon is a reference to, and named after, one of the superbosses in the game Final Fantasy VII.

entries.englishGameplaySummary: Creates a pool of poisonous goop. Stolen from the Witches' storehouse, this jar contains Essence of Poisbulon. Toxic to Gundead and Gungeoneer alike!
entries.englishEffectHighlights: Throws a vial which breaks open to create a puddle of poison goop.
entries.englishUsageNotes: If used in the Gorguns boss room, the vial will not shatter upon hitting the floor, instead sinking harmlessly into the water without releasing any poison.

entries.englishGameplaySummary: When blown, this horn will call those nearby to aid you. There are strange inconsistencies in the behavior of the Gundead. Originally thought to be heartless killing machines, they have been known to capture certain invaders for unknown purposes. Furthermore, evidence of a crude religion has been discovered. Perhaps, one day, they could be reasoned with?
entries.englishEffectHighlights: Upon use, charms enemies within an 8 tile radius around the player for 10 seconds.; Charms all merchant NPCs within an 8 tile radius of the player for 10 seconds. While charmed, the player can steal from that merchant.
entries.englishUsageNotes: Hornucopia If the player has Banana, the exploding banana will send out five bouncing explosive bananas instead of three, one of which explodes into 4 additional exploding bananas. Additionally, using Charm Horn when holding Banana fires a banana at each enemy it charms.; If used on the Misfire Beast's projection, the projection will home in on the beast and instantly kill it.

entries.unlock: Purchase from Professor Goopton for 6 HC.
entries.englishGameplaySummary: Fires poisonous rounds.
entries.englishEffectHighlights: Bullets fired from the Plague Pistol have a 50% chance to inflict the poison effect on enemies.; These bullets also leave a small pool of lingering poison goop on the ground when destroyed.
entries.englishSynergyHighlights: Pandemic Pistol If the player also has Monster Blood, Gundromeda Strain, or Irradiated Lead, the pistol turns into the Pandemic Pistol, increasing ammo capacity, and its bullets bounce off walls and are linked like VertebraeK-47's bullets.; If the player has both Pandemic pistol and the Machine Pistol with the Pistol Machine synergy, bullets from both weapons link up with each other as if shot from the same gun.
entries.englishUsageNotes: Because bullets from the Plague Pistol leave non-flammable goop on the ground, they can be used to extinguish chest fuses and the fireplace.; The entry for the Plague Pistol in the Physical Ammonomicon has a small doodle of a Mutant Bullet Kin.

entries.unlock: Purchase from Professor Goopton for 7 HC.
entries.englishGameplaySummary: Fires a stream of toxic goop. Ammo can be reused. The Syringe Gun was ordered for the Gungeon by the Médecins Sans Diplôme to perform complete blood transfusions all at once. When that proved dangerous, a crafty Gungeoneer filled the giant Syringe with a live Poisbulon.
entries.englishSynergyHighlights: Super Serum If the player also has Hazmat Suit or the Gas Mask, the Plunger's ammo capacity is increased by 50%, its projectiles can bounce, and the size of the projectiles increases.
entries.englishUsageNotes: The phrase Médecins Sans Diplôme in the description is French for Doctors Without Diplomas. This is a reference to the real-life organisation Doctors Without Borders, commonly known as Médecins Sans Frontières.; The Plunger can be seen used by The Last Human for her poison spray attack in the Robots past.

entries.englishGameplaySummary: Strangely, the needle in this compass always points toward the next descending elevator. The words "Woban, J.S.C." are engraved upon the back.
entries.englishEffectHighlights: Upon use, an arrow appears that points in the direction of the floor's exit.
entries.englishSynergyHighlights: Swift Sloop If the player also has Corsair, increases its shot speed by 50%, decreases its charge time by 33%, and decreases its reload time by 20%.
entries.englishUsageNotes: Using this item in the Resourceful Rat's Lair will cause the arrow to point in a random direction each time.; This item doesn't work in the R&G Dept., possibly because it has the same layout every time, and the player doesn't leave this floor through an elevator.

stats.value: (hit once normal) (hit twice) (hit thrice) (hit seven times rare)

entries.englishGameplaySummary: Fires a large ricochet round. In an attempt to combine the combat efficiency of bullets with the stealth and recoverability of arrows, modern archers developed the Gunbow. Though it proved ineffective in normal combat, it has taken on new life in the Gungeon.
entries.englishSynergyHighlights: Shot Across The Bow If the player has Rocket-Powered Bullets, Air Strike, iBomb Companion App, or Explosive Rounds, the Gunbow will fire rockets that explode on contact with enemies. Rockets deal 35 damage on contact and the explosion deals 35 damage. This removes the Gunbow's piercing shot.
entries.englishUsageNotes: Enemies killed by the gunbow are vaporised, and leave no corpse behind.; The name of this gun could be a reference to the Monster Hunter series, which features Bowguns.

entries.englishGameplaySummary: Movement speed increased. In an attempt to make a pair of boots that fired bullets, the wizard Alben Smallbore accidentally created these. Each step feels like being shot out of a cannon!
entries.englishEffectHighlights: Increases by 2.
entries.englishUsageNotes: Knight Time If the player has Gunknight Greaves, taking damage briefly stops time.; Triple Jump If paired with Springheel Boots, this item grants a third roll to the two already available.

entries.englishGameplaySummary: The fingerbone of an ancient lich who was skilled in the art of gunplay. Unfortunately, this fingerbone is not the lich's phylactery.
entries.englishEffectHighlights: Increases rate of fire by 25%.
entries.englishUsageNotes: Liches Get Stitches If the player has Riddle of Lead, killing an enemy grants a temporary fire rate boost, signified by a green arrow above the player's head like those above enemies buffed by a Gunsinger.; If duplicates of this item are somehow obtained, further increases manual fire rate.

entries.unlock: Carry 300 coin.
entries.englishGameplaySummary: A pre-Gungeon relic.
entries.englishEffectHighlights: Has a 10% chance to give 5 coin upon clearing a room without taking damage.
entries.englishSynergyHighlights: Great Queen Ant If the player also has Gungeon Ant, it becomes an ant queen and shoots rocket ants.; King Bomber If the player also has Lil' Bomber, its bombs become gold and spawn 0-7 coin upon hitting an enemy.
entries.englishUsageNotes: It is easy to unlock with the Briefcase of Cash. If the player already has 50 coin then the 250 will immediately unlock it.; Obtaining the Gold Junk will automatically unlock it. This is because the Gold Junk grants the player 500 coin when picked up.

entries.unlock: Purchase from Ox and Cadence for 8 HC.
entries.englishGameplaySummary: The ultimate in stealth technology. Surprise attacks deal additional damage. No one is perceptive enough to see through such an elaborate ruse.
entries.englishEffectHighlights: While wearing the box, enemies will not target the player. Most actions other than moving will remove the box, including firing a weapon, charging a weapon, dodge rolling, flipping a table, and using teleporters.; Firing a shot while under the box deals double damage and removes the box.
entries.englishSynergyHighlights: Apiary If the player has Bee Hive, it turns into an apiary and automatically regenerates ammo over time.; Flat Stanley If the player has Mailbox, the Mailbox's clip size is reduced by 50% and its reload time is reduced by 33%, allowing packages to be fired more often.
entries.englishUsageNotes: Has no effect if used while enemies are already targeting the player.; While it has no effect on regular bosses as already stated, you can use this to be hidden for the first phase of the Resourceful Rat’s boss fight.

entries.unlock: Find the secret room in the tutorial.
entries.englishGameplaySummary: The shield of Ser Manuel, emblazoned with his House sigil, the Cup of Knowledge. There is a diagram on the back illustrating how to strap on a shield and examples of when to raise it. Remarkably free of wear.
entries.englishEffectHighlights: Grants two pieces of Armor.
entries.englishSynergyHighlights: Pea Cannon If the player also has Pea Shooter, its appearance changes, damage is increased and its shots cause small explosions.; Tutorialized If the player also has Old Knight's Helm, Old Knight's Shield will orbit the player and block bullets.
entries.englishUsageNotes: This item is obtained in a secret room in the Halls of Knowledge. To find it, go to the room on the left of the first room after Manuel leaves the player. Using a blank will open the secret room where the Old Knight's Helm and the Old Knight's Shield can be found.; Talking to Ser Manuel with Old Knight's Helm and Old Knight's Shield after defeating him unlocks the Blockner fight.

entries.englishGameplaySummary: The AK-47 is in many ways the gun against which all other guns are judged.
