stats.value: 33.0 (no souls collected) 210.0 (souls collected on bosses) Infinity (souls collected non-boss)

entries.englishGameplaySummary: Can sever enemies' souls. This pocked and swollen gun splits and rends the spirit from the body.
entries.englishEffectHighlights: Consumes 6 ammo per shot, effectively having an ammo capacity of 111.; Fires fat white maggots that can pierce once.
entries.englishSynergyHighlights: Kailber k'pow uboom k'bhang If the player also has Holey Grail, Cursed Bullets, or Yellow Chamber, the split soul initially moves towards the player instead of away.
entries.englishUsageNotes: Souls cannot be stepped on during the player's I-frames after taking damage.; Stepping on a bosses soul can damage them even while they are in normally un-damageable states;

entries.unlock: Unlock almost all items and guns in the Ammonomicon
entries.englishGameplaySummary: A gun to rule them all. The swan-song of a [[Edwin
entries.englishEffectHighlights: Fires black and white bullets that are each capable of bouncing up to twice; each time the bullet bounces, the damage will be multiplied by 1.5.; The final shot in each cylinder is a larger, piercing bullet that reflects enemy bullets it comes near as it travels, but does not bounce.
entries.englishSynergyHighlights: Replete The Lower Case r's damage is increased to 5, and it can fire the words COWABUNGA, BOOYAKASHA, THANK YOU, THANKS, HASTA LA VISTA, TOODLES, GOODBYE, FAREWELL and GAME OVER.
entries.englishUsageNotes: Finished Gun actually only has an S rank, however it is programmed to replace Unfinished Gun whenever it appears, thus letting it appear out of D-tier chests.; The Finished Gun has the highest value of any gun or item in the game, due to its immense power.

entries.englishGameplaySummary: A regular shotgun. There's nothing special about it! Favored by the Shotgun Kin that roam the Gungeon, as it reminds them of their home.
entries.englishSynergyHighlights: Iron Stance If the player also has Heavy Boots, the gun's damage and knockback are increased by 20%, its shot speed is increased by 50%, and its reload time is decreased by 20%.; Not So Sawed-Off If the player also has Buzzkill, its saw blades are linked with lightning, the saw blades move slower, and it changes appearance.
entries.englishUsageNotes: The Regular Shotgun has an equip animation in which the gungeoneer racks the pump.; The Regular Shotgun is in the SHOTGUN gun class, meaning it benefits from the Shotgun Affinity synergy.

entries.englishGameplaySummary: This gun is still in the process of being forged! It barely functions in its current state, but could be a viable choice in a truly desperate situation. A small hourglass is embossed on the cylinder.
entries.englishSynergyHighlights: Phoenix Up If the player has Phoenix, while Phoenix is held, summons a phoenix companion that fires at enemies.
entries.englishUsageNotes: Despite not actually igniting enemies, the Unfinished Gun is in the FIRE gun class.; As a consequence, having the Unfinished Gun in the player's inventory makes them less likely to find other guns in the FIRE class that actually burn enemies.

entries.englishGameplaySummary: This gun is still in the process of being forged! It barely functions in its current state, but could be a viable choice in a truly desperate situation. A small hourglass is embossed on the cylinder.
entries.englishSynergyHighlights: Phoenix Up If the player has Phoenix, while Phoenix is held, summons a phoenix companion that fires at enemies.
entries.englishUsageNotes: Despite not actually igniting enemies, the Unfinished Gun is in the FIRE gun class.; As a consequence, having the Unfinished Gun in the player's inventory makes them less likely to find other guns in the FIRE class that actually burn enemies.

entries.unlock: Purchase from Doug for 26 HC.
entries.englishGameplaySummary: Fires homing rockets that erupt into short-range swarms. Comes with a helpful pal. Though this gun was built unimaginably far away, it seems it was destined to eventually find its way to the Gungeon.
entries.englishEffectHighlights: Fires an explosive missile that can pierce up to 10 enemies. When the missile impacts an enemy or obstacle, it will create 5 smaller 440 rockets that seek out enemies and explode again.; While held, grants a small robotic companion that blocks bullets and reveals the contents of nearby chests.
entries.englishUsageNotes: The Exotic is a reference to the exotic-rarity rocket launcher Gjallarhorn from Destiny, one of the most beloved and memetic exotics in the game. "Pack of Wolves" is a reference to the Gjallarhorn's exotic perk; Wolfpack Rounds.; The robot companion is a reference to the Ghosts from Destiny.

stats.value: 14.5 (15.6 with The Marine's starting Military Training)
1.2s (0.96 with The Marine's starting Military Training)
5 (4.15 with The Marine's starting Military Training)

entries.englishGameplaySummary: The Marine Sidearm was brought to the Gungeon by a low-ranking Primerdyne soldier. Though this gun appears sturdy, it has been known to fail when it is most needed.
entries.englishUsageNotes: Cannot be dropped.; Is technically automatic, as tapping the fire button does not provide a faster fire rate than holding it down.

entries.englishGameplaySummary: The Rusty Sidearm was brought to the Gungeon by the Hunter. Despite its extremely dated design and some signs of rust, this gun appears relatively new. Very strange...
entries.englishUsageNotes: Cannot be dropped.; Cannot be obtained by any Gungeoneer other than The Hunter or The Paradox.

entries.englishGameplaySummary: Just because children are young, doesn't mean they don't have pasts they would like to change. Perhaps some young soul wished to make sure his or her sibling took the blame for spilling grape juice on the couch. Much less worthy causes have been championed in the Gungeon.
entries.englishUsageNotes: The Dart Gun may be a reference to the Nerf Maverick REV-6, a toy foam dart blaster that has the same shape, similar color pattern (albeit purple instead of yellow), and also holds six darts.; The Dart Gun is a replacement toy for the Cultist, after they lost their previous toy blaster (the Light Gun) down a pit."After a clumsy child of the Gungeon accidentally dropped this toy into a pit, they were forced to amuse themselves with the Dart Gun." - The Ammonomicon description of the Light Gun

stats.value: 7 (3.5 with The Robot's starting Battery Bullets)

entries.englishGameplaySummary: The right hand of an assault robot that fled to the Gungeon. When weilded by the Robot, combines with Junk to increase all damage output. It is a simple projectile delivery system. Reliable, but its short barrel is lacking in intricacy. Under the control of a targeting AI it becomes fearsome indeed.
entries.englishUsageNotes: Cannot be dropped.; Cannot be obtained by any Gungeoneer other than The Robot or The Paradox.

entries.unlock: Defeat the High Dragun as The Bullet.
entries.englishGameplaySummary: Betrayer!
entries.englishEffectHighlights: Fires a piercing rainbow sword projectile when swung.; This projectile is only spawned if the player is at full health. The Robot is never considered at full health, and as such, cannot fire the sword projecitle.

entries.unlock: Purchase from Trorc for 25 HC.
entries.englishGameplaySummary: Place to create a zone of infinite ammo. Often found in gungeon doctors´ offices, this rack displays magazines of all sorts. The clips contained within should prove useful, and plentiful, for even the most inaccurate of Gungeoneers.
entries.englishEffectHighlights: Creates a pool of infinite ammo for a short time.; Standing inside of Magazine Rack's radius while holding an empty gun will not let you shoot using that gun. The gun must have at least 1 ammo for this to work.
entries.englishUsageNotes: Just Shoot Me If the player has Camera, this shoots at nearby enemies while active.; Junk Mail If the player has Mailbox, activating the Magazine Rack causes Mailbox to only shoot out packages.

entries.unlock: Unlock The Gunslinger.
entries.englishGameplaySummary: Once used by the lord of the Sixth Chamber as prosthetic eyes, though that may have been only for show. This pair of rounds radiates unfathomable magical power.
entries.englishEffectHighlights: Makes all guns act as if they have all of their synergies with other items or guns.
entries.englishUsageNotes: Certain synergies will not be simulated with Lich's Eye Bullets, or have additional requirements for being simulated.; Some two-component synergies that require both guns/items to function, as opposed to one or the other simply being in the player's inventory, will not be simulated. Examples and exceptions include:

entries.englishGameplaySummary: This pistol is often modified to use tranquilizer rounds. This one has not been.
entries.englishSynergyHighlights: Behold! If the player also has Com4nd0, M1911, Void Marshal, Machine Pistol, and Eye of the Beholster (the weapons the Beholster uses in game), when the Eye of the Beholster is held, the other five guns rotate around the player and shoot at nearby enemies, still using up ammo from each gun. Obtainable through the Beholster shrine, although it can be activated without.; I need scissors! 61! If the player also has Ancient Hero's Bandana, Box, Cigarettes, Ration, or Wolf, Trank Gun changes appearance, its rate of fire is decreased, damage and reload time are increased, and its bullets will inflict slow and stun. Should the player use the synergy item (such as Ration), the Trank Gun will retain the Synergy effects and appearance until using up a full magazine.
entries.englishUsageNotes: This gun is wielded by Beadies, Coalers, and the Beholster.; As it is held by the Beholster, it can be placed on the Beholster Shrine.

entries.unlock: Defeat Bullet King 10 times.
entries.englishGameplaySummary: Shoots miniature bullets on Dodge Roll. This throne was originally intended to be included with the physical edition. Due to a manufacturing defect, the scale is way off, and only suitable for a cat. It was eventually tossed into the Gungeon.
entries.englishEffectHighlights: Grants flight.; Shoots bullets around the player upon dodge rolling. This has a 2-second cooldown.
entries.englishUsageNotes: Mad Cats If the player is holding 3rd Party Controller the bullets shot from Cat Bullet King Throne home in on the enemy.; Usurper If the player has Crown of Guns, the Crown appears to fire more rapidly and also makes the Crown automatically charm Bullet Kin and Veteran Bullet Kin when held.

entries.unlock: Finish the Hollow at least 4 times and acquire Baby Good Mimic at least once in a run Retrieve Daisy for [[NPCs
entries.englishGameplaySummary: This hellish servant was lost, and hid in a chest. He's yours now. Formed from several dozen discarded shell casings, this Gundead familiar wields moderate power.
entries.englishEffectHighlights: Follows the player and attacks enemies with a green laser.; The laser does 12.5 DPS for 3.86s, with the downtime between lasers being 4.9s. The Baby Good Shelleton deals 5.5 DPS overall.
entries.englishSynergyHighlights: Birthright If the player also has Shellegun, when the gun is held, the companion will alternate between its laser and a wide-angle shot similar to the Shelleton enemy.; Shell-A-Ton If the player has Shelleton Key, Baby Good Shelleton moves and shoots much faster.
entries.englishUsageNotes: If the player stands outside the Bullet King's boss door, the Baby Good Shelleton may kill the Chancellor.; The description of the item is an altered version of the Shelleton's Ammonomicon entry.

entries.englishGameplaySummary: Powerful, but shatters upon taking damage. Normally, a gun made of glass would shatter instantaneously, but the Gungeon's energies have reinforced this inadvisable device to the point of near usability.
entries.englishEffectHighlights: Charges up to shoot a powerful piercing bolt of light.; This projectile ignores the Boss DPS cap.
entries.englishSynergyHighlights: Max Pane If the player has Glass Guon Stone, Glass Cannon will change appearance and will not break if the player takes damage. Instead, the Guon Stones will be destroyed, losing the synergy but granting one extra hit before it breaks.
entries.englishUsageNotes: While shattered (empty due to taking damage), the Glass Cannon has no sprite, effectively making it invisible.; If the Glass Cannon is emptied by using up its ammo reserve instead of taking damage, the gun remains unshattered and can be dropped and retrieved like any other. It can still be shattered if held while taking damage.

entries.englishGameplaySummary: Creates a temporary second you. Though the quality of this clone is debatable, Believe It! or not, this clone has seen extensive use in the Gungeon. Not to be confused with the Clone. While the Clone provides a second chance at life, the Shadow Clone will merely laugh at you upon your demise.
entries.englishEffectHighlights: Upon use, spawns a temporary clone of the player.; The clone's movements are opposite to the player.
entries.englishUsageNotes: Kinjutsu If the player also has Decoy, Shadow Clone spawns three clones.; Due to the nature of this synergy, it requires an inventory-expanding item such as Utility Belt or Backpack.

entries.unlock: Purchase from Doug for 26 HC.
entries.englishGameplaySummary: Shows combat data. Increases accuracy and damage by a small amount. This scouter, worn with use, provides detailed data on enemies encountered within the Gungeon. The name "Ritvik" is inscribed on the rim.
entries.englishEffectHighlights: Shows enemy health bars. Dealing damage to enemies will trigger a number pop-up showing how much damage was dealt.; Decimal damage numbers will be rounded to the nearest whole integer in their pop-up.
entries.englishUsageNotes: Mini bosses will not show their health bar.; The Scouter can make spotting the original Killithid when they split easy as it will still show the damage dealt to the original.

entries.unlock: Defeat the boss of the R&G Dept.
entries.englishGameplaySummary: These bullets Zero in on their target and keep slashing. Legend tells of a katana bullet so quick and skilled that it once sliced through a bullet molecule in mid air.
entries.englishEffectHighlights: Increases curse by 1.; Upon killing an enemy, bullets have a chance to unleash a flurry of slashes along the path of the original bullet, damaging enemies.
entries.englishUsageNotes: Because the flurry pushes enemies and players around, using Katana Bullets can damage the player in indirect and unpredictable ways.; Companions that damage enemies can trigger the effects of Katana Bullets. Due to this, players should exercise caution to avoid being pushed into hazards by the blade-flurries created by their companions.

entries.unlock: Give health to the Vampire 3 times.
entries.englishGameplaySummary: A wooden beam, often used in construction. Not technically a gun, but you could probably smack some Bullet Kin around with it.
entries.englishEffectHighlights: While the trigger is held, this gun will extend in the form of a stiff beam until reaching the end of its range.; Swinging the beam into enemies deals considerably more damage and knockback than direct hits.
entries.englishSynergyHighlights: Pea Cannon If the player also has Pea Shooter, Pea Shooter shots become larger, deal more damage, and explode.
entries.englishUsageNotes: The Wood Beam deals no damage to chests. For this reason, it can be an excellent way to check for Mimics. Its length can also bypass locked doors, to check if the chest inside is a mimic.; Wood Beam's damage multiplier from swing speed applies after Unity's flat damage bonus. This makes Unity especially effective with Wood Beam, as Unity's damage bonus will be multiplied by 2, 8, 16, or 26 depending on how fast the beam is swung.
